situation: It is not well to do better information for gather with my friends and they irritated me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, can i help today? Please, tell me about yourself?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hi i'm nereida

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, do have any issues that you would like to share with me today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i am in disputed mod with my friends

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that. So, you and your friends are not seeing eye to eye? Do you mind telling me what the issue is that yo are not agreeing on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I explained my personal problems but they are not giving proper response

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That can be difficult and I can relate to that too. When friends do not respond the way that we feel that they should it can be really hard. Do you have any family that you are close with?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They need more money from me, but i couldn't  give  that much amount

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand the issue of money can cause a lot of problems in a relationship. I had a best friend borrow money once and it lead to her never paying it back and we stopped being friends. Do you plan on trying to talk to your friends soon? Maybe I can give you some advice on how to approach them.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I want to speak with them because they are in good nature

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe you could chose a place to meet that is neutral, somewhere at a park or at a cafe. You can ask them to reflect on the good memories in your friendship and then express how you value them as people and friends. Does that sound like something you can try?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, i want to do some moderate friendship with them

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a good idea too. Having boundaries is the healthiest part of a relationship so that neither party gets taken advantage of. Setting up lines not to cross is a great idea.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I want to do some help to them but i don't do expect more from them

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand that friends can disappoint us sometimes and friendships can take a lot of effort. I would recommend starting a journal. A daily journal can help you express your feelings on paper and it can help you from getting to frustrated and keeping your feelings inside.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, some disappointments show us about real face of friends, so it was an experience to me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am proud of you today because you reached out and have tried to find help outside of yourself. You are growing and trying to do your best.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Sure, it is my new way of relationship to me with others

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, you're growing and trying to do your best and wanting to expand your boundaries and make them clear. You have taken the right approach and are on the best track to having a successful relationship in the future. Great Job! Thank you for sharing your story with me today.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Great, thank you friend

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are welcome and here for you anytime. I am able to listen and do my best to help you navigate friendships and other issues too. We are always here.

situation: I was homeless. I had to leave a place I loved. It was mentally a bad state.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do not know what to do. I feel like shit.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay try not to worry. Can you tell me a bit about why you feel so bad?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I got kicked out of the home I was living in.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow I am really sorry. Were you living with family, or with friends?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I lived in a rented room.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you were thrown out from a place you were renting? Did you do anything wrong?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No. The renter said he just wanted me out. He also charged me ninety extra dollars.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow that is illegal. Do you know that there are organisations that can help you with this sort of thing?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No. I did not. I asked my exboyfriend at the time for advice. He just say to pay him and move.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that that was bad advice to be honest. I think that you are being ripped off. Do not worry, there are things that you can do about it!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is reassuring. I would like action to be taken for justice.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are definitely entitled to it. Do you have somewhere else to live in the meantime?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have a couch to crash on. Another guy said I could store my furniture in his basement. But no official room.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It's a precarious position to be in, especially during a pandemic. Do you have any family members who might help?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I could call my mother. She has a room to stay in. But I only have the money to drive to her home, not the money for a rental truck to move all my stuff.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Would any family members be prepared to loan you money or a vehicle?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I could ask my aunt to use her car, she has an SUV, where as I drive a sedan.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How much stuff do you have that needs to be moved?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Queen bed, bed frame, small desk, roll chair, bookshelf, rug, boxes of kitchen stuff, etc.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Try not to worry too much - I was in this position once myself and it did all turn out for the best though it was very scary at the time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I feel almost numb from the depression. Like non of my stuff matters, only that I am physically safe.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it would be a good idea for you to try and get medical help for your depression.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I agree. Asking for help is a plan.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I wish you all the very best with it - there is help out there!  Enjoy the rest of your day :)

situation: My boyfriend just told me that he wants to break up because he doesn't see a future because he never wants to get married but I do.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi, Thanks for talking to me today. I've been so upset about my boyfriend breaking up with me.  It was so unexpected.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that. When did he break up with you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: He texted me yesterday!  Can you believe that?  We've been together a year and he broke up with me by texting me!!!  He couldn't even do it face to face!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That seems very cowardly to break up with you over text after being together for a year.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I thought so too!  He knew I'd be upset and he didn't want to deal with me I guess.  He knew I wanted to get married some day from the very start.  He knew it didn't have to be soon.  I'm still in college.  I just want to know that he wants marriage some day but he decided that marriage is an institution he doesn't believe in.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a shame. I can understand his perspective if marriage is not something he wanted but it seems hurtful to string you along in  a relationship if your life goals were very different. Was your differing opinions on marriage the only reason he gave?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, It was completely unexpected because things with us were going great.  I was completely blindsided.  We hadn't even talked about marriage lately.  He just knew that it was something that I wanted in the future.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds very difficult. How have you been coping with the break up?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've just been crying and laying in bed.  I called off work today.  I don't want to see anyone right now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think this is to be expected from a very fresh break-up. I know that when I broke up with my partner, I spent at least a few days crying and trying to figure out what my life would be now.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: How 'd you get over it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I tried to focus on other areas of my life and to critically reflect on my relationship. Largely, I threw myself into my work and tried to better that aspect of my life, and hung out with people that I knew cared about me. Both of those are very good distractions which you might be able to use.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Maybe calling off work was a bad idea...  all I'm going to do is think about this instead of doing something to take my mind off of it...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think one day off to get the crying out of your system is a good emotional cleanse. We all need time to self care and look after our emotions. Maybe going for a walk or doing some yoga, to place your focus on something rather than being boxed into a room may help to shift your focus.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I think I'm going to get out of bed and do something to take my mind off of this .  Thanks for chatting with me today!
 I think I'm going to do that!  There is a park by my house that I'm going to go for a walk in!  Some fresh air might be good for me!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are more than welcome. I hope that you feel better by being outside.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks!  I hope so too!

situation: i have depression because recently i lost my job

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, can I help you today? Would you like to share how you're feeling?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm in depression because recently i lost my job

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am so sorry to hear that you lost your job. Did you work there for a long time?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes up to five years but my company getting too much lost so they took out lots of employs

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand that being used to a routine and then suddenly losing that can cause a lot of depression and confusing for you. Are you feeling lost?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i feel very depress

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am proud of you taking the step to reach out today and share your feelings with me. That takes a lot of strength on your part.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for understand my feelings , do you have any experience like that

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome and yes I had a similar situation about a year ago. I had worked for the same company for 10 years and they laid me off. I was blindsided and very upset and felt depressed also. I started looking for jobs and found a better one that paid more, so it worked out for the best in the end.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: what you think if apply for new job , i will come out from this depression?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand that you are feeling a deep sadness and a situational depression. I believe that when you takes steps forward and start applying for jobs then you will see a lot of opportunities are out there. Do you have any family that can be supportive to you through this difficult time?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for support

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome. Often sometimes local charities will help with putting together a resume and helping find you a job. Maybe you could reach out for assistance in creating a new resume?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: do you have any idea for getting new job?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would recommend looking for an local group that help assist creating new resumes. Nonprofits have people who volunteer.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for sharing your ideas

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome, I would also suggest reaching out to your friends and family for support during this challenging time. You are a faithful employee and have a great future ahead.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes they are supporting but its difficult situation

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You can always reach out to me and I can providing  you a place to vent and express yourself and the feelings that you are having. Hopefully you feel like a plan to begin apply for new jobs is a start forward.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you so much for you support , i fell better

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you for being so strong and reaching out today. Have a great rest of your day.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thanks

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would be happy to continue if you have anything else you would like to say and of not kindly use the feature to finishing this session.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i don't have anything else , please finish

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you, the feature is provided to you and you alone. The button is for you to confirm you have finished and quit the session and would like to continue with your day. However, I am happy to talk as long as you are if you don't want to do that, I am unable to on my end.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: sorry is your side

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you, but it is actually red and located under your blue button feature. Thanks again for your time today.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes from your side

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand however that is not the case. I do not have the feature that will submit and therefore complete. Please use the red button that saying finish/quit in order to proceed.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: ok

situation: I have been feeling kinda down lately since my pet died. I miss her so much.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello how is your day today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Hello. Not so good.
 I lost my cat recently. It surely feels... overwhelming at times.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a very hard thing to be going through where you very close to your cat?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, I had her for 11 years.
 She died of natural causes.
 I lived alone with her. The house feels extremely empty now...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I am very sorry to hear that i have also lost a pet earlier this year his name was fish and was only 3 years old he unfortunately passed due to liver cancer. It was a very trying experience but slowly the pain of his passing has got a little bit easier.
 Have you tried talking about this loss to friends of family?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No. My family lives in another country, and I really don't have any close friends.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What have your feelings been like after the passing of your cat?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel hopeless. Alone. I feel like a chunk of myself passed along with her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Those are completely valid feelings to have at this time Have you considered getting a small reminder of your cat perhaps a small shrine or tribute to him/her? Her I apologize for the confusion.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No. I fear that things like that may remind me of her and make me feel worse.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Expressing your emotion is very healthy for example maybe taking up a new hobby or trying something new that scares or excites you to get your mind off of this difficult time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I used to play videogames before. Now I'm not so sure I would enjoy them.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What was your favorite genre of video games?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: First person shooters and MMORPG's.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you considered playing again possibly joining an online team and competing in tournaments?

situation: I am very scared about next college semester it is frightening

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how can I help you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would like some help with the problem I am facing

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: OK, sure. Can you tell me what the problem is? I'll do my best to help.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well I am going into my next college semester next month, and I am very frightened about a calculus class I have to take. It's an honors course and I am very worried that I will not do well

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand that. It must be an important exam for you. Do you enjoy calculus? I'm terrible at anything with numbers, myself!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't remember, I took an easy calculus course in high school but that was a couple years ago. I only got a B there, so I'm worried about taking an honors one. I have to take it for my degree goal

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: A B is a great result! Are there any revision courses that you can access to help refresh your skills?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't think so, the campus is closed due to the pandemic. There are a few online tutors but they are almost always busy

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: This pandemic has really made it difficult to get anything done hasn't it. Do you know of anyone else who is looking to take the course that you got revise together with? I always find it easier to work through things like this with someone else.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes the pandemic has affected many of my friends and their families, very difficult these times are.
 No, none of my friends are taking the class/ none of them have ever taken calculus

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There seem to be some really good online courses out there. It might be worth sending out enquiries to a number of tutors, asking them to get in touch as soon as they are free, and in the meantime working through something online to help build up your confidence?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
That sounds like some good advice to me
 Maybe I'll try working on it for my confidence like you said

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I bet once you get into it again it will all come back to you. Sometimes getting started is the hardest part.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I think you're right!
 Do you believe in me? That I will pass well?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that if you got a B once, you can do it again, and if you really want it, which you clearly do, you can do anything! Aim for an A, you can do it!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thank you very much you've been very helpful
 God bless you have a good day

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And you too, good luck!

situation: My partners and I are not on the same terms. Need some emotional support.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
HI Anyone there?
 Hello? Is anyone there?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Hi there I am so sorry
 was a little distracted but I am here.  How are you feeling?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, it's a weird story. I am feeling very sad about it.
 I think that the person just changed over time. We grew apart.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that.  Do you want to tell me the story? I promise it won't be weird. I've been through a lot.   Oh yeah?  Was this a friend, partner, family member?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My boyfriend just stopped communicating with me. It's like he lost interest in me and moved on. Breakups are the worse around the holidays. It was around Thanksgiving last year. Have you ever been through a breakup during the holidays?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am so sorry to hear that.  Have you tried anything to address the situation?  Perhaps bringing it up to him subtlly?  Or even just asking how HE is, to see if he has siomething he wants to talk about.  Oh goodness, yes.  I actually was ghosted by the woman I thought was my soulmate... on my birthday.  It was... strategic of her.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I tried to call, text, and email him but he never responded. I just accepted it as he just moved on, and I had to do the same.  I am sorry to hear about your girlfriend. That must have been hard to experience your breakup on your birthday.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you haven't heard from him since the end of November?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, he just totally cut off all communication. I don't know whether or not he is safe or anything.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there a family member or trusted friend you could reach out to?  Has he logged into or posted on any of his social media accounts?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: We never went out on a date or anything. It was just strictly communicating my phone, text, and email. It was kind of weird, like he was hiding something. I don't know about any of his friends or family. He does not have any social media accounts.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is he close to you or is this a long distance type thing?  Just for context's sake. No judgment of course.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It was more of a long distance relationship. It is far to love from a distance though. It is hard to love from a distance-- sorry.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, it can also be... easier to open up to someone and the relationships can be very intimate, even from a distance.  So your feelings are valid, 100%.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes. It was nice talking to you. I am about to sign off. Have a great day!

situation: I am battling with letting my past childhood trauma effect my relationship and the raising of my child.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello. I am battling with my childhood trauma that i feel is affecting my everyday life with my child and spouse. I don't know how to tell my spouse about my childhood in fear he doesn't want to be with someone with a upbringing such as mine.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, thank you for chatting with me. From what I understand, you are struggling with childhood trauma and this is affecting everyday life with your child and spouse.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, correct. I am not sure how to tell my spouse about my childhood trauma. He wants to know. He wants to help me. But I am not sure where to begin with explaining it to him.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a really hard situation to be in, but I know that you can have that conversation with your spouse! Does he have any idea about any of the trauma you have experienced?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am worried it will effect his views on me. And he has a very rough idea of things that have happened.. he tells me he wants to know everything so he can help me move forward.. but when i try to talk about it i ramble and end up not explaining anything. I have even tried writing it down.. and nothing seems to work.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Just listening to you talk I can tell this is something that is making you extremely anxious.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: very much so.. whenever it gets brought up i free and begin to ramble and i think it makes him overwhelmed ..

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: One thing I would suggest is after writing everything down that you rehearse it for a while in front of a mirror and picture that you are talking to your husband while you are doing this.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
He had a very different childhood then myself. Almost complete opposite then myself. And I have tried... i cant seem to get my self to even put my past down on paper... 
 When I have tried to i end up sending my self into a panic.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you reached out to other people or a group of people who may have experienced the same type of trauma you have?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No.. i do not know many people fro my area. As soon as i was able to leave where i grew up i packed up and left. We live in a extremely rural area where there is not many new faces.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have also experienced childhood trauma in my past and it was extremely hard to get through. I found talking to people who went through the same thing helped a lot.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Maybe i will have to look int finding support group in my area...thank you very much!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: After speaking with others in the same situation, I was able to convey this to my loved ones in a better way and I was much more comfortable with it,

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you for your help!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are very welcome! Thank you for chatting with me today!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You did a great job!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad I could help, if you ever need anything else don't be afraid to contact us back!

situation: I have to find a higher paying job but it has been a while since I worked full time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there, how are things with you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hey, I'm doing well, how are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am not so bad. Is there anything you'd like to discuss with me today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I am looking for emotional support. I need to find a higher paying job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you not happy in your current job? It's a bad time to be job hunting really.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I was unemployed all year but that is no longer an option.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What sort of work are you looking for? Would you be prepared to take something outside of your usual field?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I want to do avoid labor jobs as I have other talents, but I'm not totally against it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sometimes it is necessary to take a temporary job that you don't really like just to make ends meet alas.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yea, but I worry that it will be a waste a time that could have been spent towards building a career.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well true but if there's no work out there that will help build your career you kind of don't have much choice ;)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Perhaps I should focus on what would be most fun to do.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you thought of taking some sort of refresher or training course? There are quite a few available.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What would you recommend?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hmmm well how are your computing skills? That's a great field to be in at the moment.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am good with computers but not fluent in any programming languages.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well that would be an interesting thing to learn don't you think? Though I don't know much about them myself I must admit.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It would take a lot of effort on the front end before you ever got paid.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It would but if you've nothing better to do due to the pandemic it might be worth it  Have you considered registering with employment agencies for temp work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It would be nice to have something reliable long term, but I have done temp work in the past.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Me too. It can be a bit soulless but it does pay the bills when you don't have anything else to do.  Anyway I hope that I have been able to help you a little and that you are feeling a little more positive about things?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Absolutely. It is encouraging to know that I can get out there and work for anyone.

situation: I'm addicted to marijuana and cannot stop consuming it on a daily basis.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there, how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am doing well. My marijuana consumption today has been less than what it has been for the last week.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well that is good. Are you trying to come off it completely?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Absolutely not. I'm not even in California yet, where I know my usage will explode. It's just difficult managing the realities of quarantine with the effects of the marijuana

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, do you worry about your consumption? If you don't I don't see it as a problem :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, because of the financial strain it places on me and my wife, who is also a regular user.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah yes, it is not cheap. I am not sure how much to suggest as it's not legal in my country :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It was recently legalized in our state. My wife and I were actually one of the first people in line to buy it legally last year, before the pandemic. It is a major part of our lives.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am in the UK. It's sort of a grey area ... police won't really do anything but you can't buy it over the counter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: One of the best things about legalization has been the expurgation of criminal records for incarcerated people jailed on marijuana charges. I'm very grateful that these atrocities have been rectified at least partially.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes I would be too. Even twenty five years ago in my student days it was rare for anyone to be prosecuted for pot.  The US seems to be really strict on it tho

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It varies, state by state, depending on which party is governing. Which you of course have a version of in the UK. Mayoral elections were today yeah?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: They were but I didn't pay much attention :) I vote in the Generals and the locals but that's all .

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Oh, well, I'm sorry to hear that. I think participation in local governance is actually the most achievable path for change, but perhaps how it's structured where you are, it wouldn't really matter much if you participated.
 I guess mayorals are only technically local, not like parliamentary elections

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If I were in London or a major city I'd vote for Mayor. I am in a rural area and we don't have one :D. I vote for local councillors tho

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Jeremy Corbyn, no surrender?

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
You sound like someone I'd like to smoke a spliff with
In a moderated manner of course, in relevance to the topic I opened with

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: But I don't get to vote for London mayor. Only if I were a London resident or nearby would I (sorry for tortured syntax)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Prescriptive grammar is fascism, use whatever words in whatever order you like
 Ironic given that I just watched My Fair Lady the other day

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Haha I don't indulge these days, except for once in a blue moon :P

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You've got those weird drug shops in the UK, with synthetics and all that

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Not where I am :( All we have here is sheep and cows.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Let me guess - north/eastern UK?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: North West but close :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have a slightly deeper knowledge of the UK than most Americans. I binged Derry Girls in 2 days so my expertise is almost professorial here

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: haha!  I am not touching that one :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It's a good series but yeah if you don't skew younger demographic wise I could see why you'd avoid it. You more of a Last Tango In Halifax type?
 Don't even watch the Circle?
 Only Connect?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
True that
 But, you know
 Legally

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker

I hope one day your people find liberation
I think you have helped me a lot with my marijuana problem

Yes, have a wonderful evening!
 Goodbye

Hello Gday
 How are you tiday?

dialog.content:
Thank youu

 Not really spoken to anyone

 and feeling entitled

dialog.speaker: supporter

Yea i know right

Certainly did

forward to better relationship both sides in the future

Thank you so much for listening
  Absolutely

situation: I don't love my partner any more and I don't know what to do for the best as she really loves me but it's not working.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello! How are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I've been better to be honest. I think I need to break up with my girlfriend but she moved in last year and I don;'
 know how to approach it. I think I'm going to be in for a bad emotional time

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That seems like a tough situation! What feelings made you decide you need to breakup with her?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's a bit of a mix. I don't find her attractive that much any more and I think that's important. Also since moving in she is difficult to live with. I can't see myself with her long term.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I completely understand that. I recently broke up with my boyfriend earlier this year, because I didn't feel like I loved him as much as I used to. Things like that are important! 
 How long have you guys been together?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's good that you have had a similar experience and understand what I'm going through. We dated for a while. The thing is she is Italian and has moved countries to live with me. I feel so bad that this has happened.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Oh I see. That's a huge step to make in a relationship. So you are hesitant to break up with her because she moved just to be with you?
 Have you communicated with her about your concerns? Such as being difficult to live with?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes that's right, I helped her get a job and thought I'd just see how it goes. Her family loves me and thinks I'm great which makes this even more difficult. We have talked about things yes but she sees it as a bit of a joke I think.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it's great that we got matched up, because I went through also the exact same situation. My boyfriend and I were long distance, and he moved across the country to be closer to me. So when I felt like we needed to break up, I was experiencing similar guilt that you are experiencing.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It's great that you understand this. I think being the man puts more pressure on me to look after her and make sure she
 she's safe etc

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I want to reassure you, that doing what's best for you is what is most important! Your partner will understand this if they truly care for you. Things will be tough for a while, but you both will be able to grow from this experience. And just because you don't want to be in a relationship anymore, doesn't mean that you don't care about her! 
 Staying friends after the fact might take the pressure off of you a little bit. You can still help her out and be there for each other, but as friends instead of partner's.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: To be honest, it's amazing that you have suggested this. It's really hard to balance my needs and feelings without breaking her heart. It's hard for the person that's dumped but actually I think it is equally hard for the dumper!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Yes exactly! It's so difficult for the dumper as well, because of course you still have strong feelings for this person. They just aren't romantic anymore. 
 And that is okay. You don't need to feel guilty for things that you can't control. You can still be there for her without pretending to be in love

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's exactly it. So do you think I should just talk to her and tell her how I feel? The thing is it's not like she can move out immediately. It's going to be a long drawn out experience.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think talking to her and being transparent about your feelings is definitely the right move. In doing this, reassure her you still want to be friends and you aren't just "kicking her to the curb." Things will obviously be awkward for the first little bit, because continuing to live together will bring it's own difficulties. But the beautiful thing about that is, you guys are already used to it. :) So it's only hard if you make it hard

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I think I need to emphasize the friends element of it and potentially support her to move on. I suppose I'm fearful that it will never end but I'll need to face into that and stay strong, and obviously avoid being intimate as this would really hurt her if I continued to sleep with her. I need to be careful about that I think!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes exactly. And from chatting with you, I can tell that you are a very caring person. You don't want to hurt her. I believe that you have the understanding of the situation, and you know what you should and shouldn't do in order to make it work. The hard part is simply getting the courage to finally discuss it. But I think you are going to be able to do that just fine!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I must say, you have been fantastic to talk to about this, we have had a similar experience so I think the advice is coming from experience. Thanks for that!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
What I've gathered about you as a person, makes me confident that you probably chose a partner that will be able to have this talk with you and you both will come to an understanding. 
 Yes I think it's very great we were able to connect on our issues! It definitely helps. You are welcome! :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for a great conversation. To continue as is wouldn't be fair on her and I see that now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Of course! And it wouldn't be fair to her, just as much as it wouldn't be fair to you. Your happiness is just as important!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah, I need to put myself first but also be considerate of her feelings. Thanks for that
 Take care and enjoy your day.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Of course! I'm glad that you are feeling a bit better about everything! You as well!

situation: COVID-19 made me lose my beautiful job which I admire a lot and I am searching for another one which is not fort coming. Please what can I do now?

situation:
Cause of the covid, I have problems with study and communication. 
I have problem in my University, also problems how to communicate with people after 1 year, of lockdown.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, glad to meet you virtually. How are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Im doing fine. What about you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am good.  How may I help you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I have problem with the communication. One of the main problems, is that Im not sociable person, so I dont have a lot of friends, and the situation with Covid, just finished me. Now, I'm siitin at my home, just playing game and study. How can I start to communicate with people? 
 Or to meet the new peoples?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I completely get that.  I am thinking that most people are feeling isolated therefore communication with others are feeling even more challenging thank usual.  Playing games can certainly help.  What type of games do you play?  Perhaps there are people that share the same interests

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
For example, the games that I'm playing are: Call of Duty, League of Legends. Yes it's multiplayer game, where can communicate throught the discord or voice, but I'm making a lot of cringe staff, that takes away new playes/friends. 
 I guess, it cause of my fears, that I can miss the new friends.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Multi player games help I would assume.  Do you play with people you actually know sometimes?  I am sure that you make a great friend so we can explore areas of meeting new people,  I can tell you that I am not having the easiest time keeping up with friends and I am certainly not meeting new people in person very often myself.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Almost, I play solo. Yeah, I undertsand your message. You said right things.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay so perhaps play with people as opposed to solo.   Do you think it would be worth a try?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I need to try it. I guess it's one of the best way to solve my problem. Thanks

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are quite welcome.  Perhaps I can play as well.  Is it easy to catch on or are the games you play difficult?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yh, it kinda difficult, cause need to know some staff, before you play it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Got it! Do you play sports or any other activities where you encounter people?

situation: I lost my job recently, and it has been very difficult to keep paying my bills.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Quite bad. I tried to send some job applications but there are still no reply.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hear you. It's been a terrible year all round for job seekers, given the global situation at the moment. I have been there myself.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Can you give me some advice on how to cope with this?  I honestly don't know what to do.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, firstly I think you need to accept that this is not your fault and that at the moment the world situation is beyond your control.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I understand, but still,
 emotionally it is hard to cope.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you able to cope financially whilst you are out of work?  Perhaps you could  take on some gig work just to earn a little extra spare cash?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Not really, my savings are running out. I am doing some part-time freelance work but it won't pay all the bills.
 Do you have any suggestions?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. I have been in the same situation and I know how frightening it is. Have you though of consulting agencies such as food banks that can help you temporarily?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I haven't been to food banks yet. I can still deal with food on the table for now, but a couple more months without job then
 I might have to.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you feel that you can at least manage for a few more weeks?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Financially yes. It is just that my emotion is getting worse and worse. Stress I guess.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it might be a good idea for you to consult your doctor? It sounds as though you might be suffering from depression.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I don't know. Psychologist appointments are very expensive in my country.
 And without a job I do not have any insurance.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that. I have had therapy but it is free in my country. Are there any charities that might offer a limited amount of counselling time?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have not heard of charities that does that. If I am suicidal, may be, but I guess they are not dealing with stress from normal job seekers...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Would you be prepared to take on a job that is outside of  your normal field and income bracket, just temporarily?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would. I work as an IT consultant before, but I would take any job for now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know that it is very difficult with so many businesses being forced to close. Would you be prepared to do delivery work, perhaps?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Do you mean like food delivery?  like UberEats?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes. Or most restaurants are offering take out services at the moment so there will be delivery services out there

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I guess I could try that, not sure it pays well enough. 
 But thank you for your help.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are most welcome. I hope that things will pick up come the new year and you can get back to your regular profession! Just try to think of this as a stop gap

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I hope so as well.  Thank you a lot for advice, I feel better just talking about it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad to have been of help .. enjoy the rest of your day!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too, hope you have a good day.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You have to end it from your end .. I can't :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I see, alright, see you then!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: just hit quit and take the survey :)

dialog.content: hello

dialog.content: Hi there

dialog.content: It's ok.  I am trying to get my crafting business off the ground.  I'm just stressed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It is a very hard time to get a new business going, isn't it? Are you having problems due to the pandemic?  I have also been having financial woes at this time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No just keeping motivated.  I sell wreaths but I'm switching more to tumblers (just easier storage wise).

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well it's great that you've got a business venture! Do you have experience with this sort of thing?

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I need more motivation, time and energy.
 I have not but that's a good idea

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel like I need more hands/arms too.  If I was like an octopus I'd be good lol

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: LOL so you feel like you just don't have enough help to complete all your orders? Could you hire an assistant?

situation: Had to work till 11pm and up early the next morning. No emotional support and completely drained of all energy. Have no desire to continue with the job, but need to get paid.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi! How are you tonight?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Im ok - tired. Yourself.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm doing fairly well, Thanks. Can I encourage you or support you in some way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes - definitely. I am not sure how to proceed or what to do in my situation. I am exhausted, mentally and physically.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You sound as if you are in a slump. I've been there. Perhaps defining the problem and some solutions can help?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Perhaps - i dont know the solution. the problem is I cannot continue to work these hours - it is just not sustainable.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What are your hours?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: like generally, or currently in these situations? usually its around 9-5 (give or take).  These days its 9 - 7 or 11pm or later.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have to say, when I am physically and mentally exhausted, I don't think as clearly and lose hope more easily.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: right, and that is the issue of where I am at.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: 9 to 11 would exhaust me. That is 14 hours?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yeah - something like that. and lunches are at the desk - as i am working from home - so its pretty much no breaks either.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would certainly agree with you. That kind of work is unsustainable. Let me ask you this. What would happen if you told your employer you simply could not do these hours?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hah - i dunno. im on a team of 2 people - and i guess if i dont do it, the other person has to. or it just doesnt get done, and i dont know what happens?? nothing? LOL.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: The worst that could happen is that they fire you. But that doesn't seem too likely as they seem to need you. But I have learned in life that if I don't care for myself, hardly anyone does. We need to be kind to ourselves. Set boundaries.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: agreed - i dont think they would fire me, maybe get reprimanded or more likely a guilt trip as to why I cant do it. Im just not sure what kind of boundaries I set when we have things due, and just literally no time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Setting boundaries can be difficult if you haven't done that a lot. When we think we don't have the ability to say NO, then we feel trapped, worn out, depressed, taken advantage of. People need to know that we reasonably can say no.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, I have to set boundaries, because working till 11pm most nights is not sustainable. and who on earth is going to view, let alone sign a contract at 12am

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that would be the first step. Determine how many hours you feel you COULD work. Then advise them that is what you will do. Find out what kind of timelines there are, and if you can't do it, don't go beyond your boundaries. It is obviously destroying your life. It really isn't healthy for you. Exactly. It isn't working for you. Something has to change. And you sound intelligent, so you can determine what will work for you -- perhaps a range of hours, but definitely not 14 hours. Unless you are a young kid. lol Even then, that's a lot.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yeah - ill come up with reasonable boundaries till 5:30 is reasonable, and if its a do or die situation cap it at 7:30 --- and thats only for dire situations. if that doesnt work, well then i guess either i dont have a job, or people figure out how to work in regular hours.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds reasonable to me. And if they try to make you feel guilty, reject that. Tell them, I'm sorry, but I feel this is reasonable, and truly it is.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hah i used to go out like a young kid, but still - just because most of us are working from home 24/7, doesnt mean I actually can work 24/7. even fi i were young, hell no. they dont pay me enough.  thanks i really appreciate it. it sounds reasonable, and its what a coworker told me. if i dont set boundaries, i will crack.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No one can work 24/7.  I think they may not even be aware you are struggling. So communication is important.   You don't want to crack. It's not worth it.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yeah, i think a call to not just my boss is in order, but also HR to advise them of the situation. they need to start telling managers, we are NOT front of the line workers, nor police, fire, etc. our jobs are not THAT important.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I will say a prayer that you communicate your needs politely but firmly. And not believe false guilt. As you said, you aren't putting out fires and doing CPR.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: plus netflix, hulu, amazon keep releasing new shows. if i need a reason, it can simply be to watch tv and detox (so to speak) and get away from the BS of work (obv stating it more nicely than that).

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: This has been a stressful year for many of us, you don't need to be working yourself into depression and exhaustion.  You really do not NEED a reason not to work unreasonable hours.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: agreed 100% --- ive already hit those points, and it is not good for anyone let alone my job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It isn't anyone's business what you do on your off hours.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: completely agreed! and thanks for listening  :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I do suggest you simply stick to the facts. These are the hours, I can't work more.  You don't need to explain that to anyone. But be firm and polite. And you are very welcome. Sometimes we just need a friend! :D

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: its what i needed to hear from someone who is more anonymous (so to speak). who doesnt have a first hand attachment to me, so its a great assessment.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I've been in that boat before. It isn't easy, but I learned what I had to do, and I don't get in that boat anymore. And I can see to help others get out.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
:-)
firm and direct is the best approach. esp for my boss. she will appreciate that and then i will stick to what i say!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Amen!  GREAT!  I'm glad to hear it!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: cheers! take care and have a great day. if you dont do this in your day job, you should consider it. or at least i get the feeling youre there for others

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you! I'm retired but do community work! :D Am considering getting an office. I do love to encourage! YOu have a blessed day!

situation: I had my heart set on a Veterinary Technology program at a particular school and I just found out that I won't be able to apply in time. I feel like I have to reassess my future plans.

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: supporter

situation: I fear my child is in bad company. Recently his behavior has changed to the worst. I don't know how to approach the issue.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello my name is Sharon, how are you today? :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi Sharon. Am good just concerned about my son.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh I see.  What's happening with your son?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I suspect my son is in bad company. His most recent behaviour is so different from what I am used to

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh that's hard to deal with.   My daughter ran with a bad crowd for a while and I remember the deep worry. What sort of behaviour are you noticing?  How old is he?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My son is 15. Recently he's keeping to himself and mostly stays locked in his room. He barely allows anyone in.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And when you try and talk to him about it what does he say?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: He says he's studying online. But I suspect he's doing other stuff online apart from study

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh I see.  Apart from isolating how is he doing otherwise, like at school?  Is he still seeing his friends at all? It can be scary not knowing what our kids are up to online.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not really. He just has this one particular friend who comes over sometimes and they lock themselves in the room.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hmm.  So a lot of mystery.   I can understand you being worried.   Is he your only child?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am so scared I don't want to confront him I have always tried to show him how much I trust him as away to make him feel responsible enough Yes. He is my only child and I am single parent

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thats good parenting!   Perhaps if you frame it as being about you, e.g. "I am getting really worried, can you please explain....it's affecting me (affecting my sleep/work/whatever" .... maybe it will inspire him to be more forthcoming Any signs of depression?   I have been depresed before, and self-isolating can be a symptom, I've been there...

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Never thought of it that way. That could be a possibility. But he always seems happy and we chat a lot onlythat he his behind closed doors in his room for a long time everyday.
 Am also concerned about the friend who comes over. I don't really know his family or home just that he's from the neighborhood and they go to the same school.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hmm.  My other thought is tell him what you just told me, that you have been trying to show him that you trust him....but be honest and tell him you are afraid.   Hopefully, he won't want you feeling that way, and will open up a bit. Yes I can see that being concerning when you know nothing about the friend.   Yet you walk a fine line because he needs the freedom and autonomy to choose his friends without explanation required...it's such a balancing act.   I can tell you that I have raised a daughter, and that teen years were tougher than toddlerhood.   Lots of worry, but she came through it and is fine now. I wonder if there is a teacher he is close to that you could bring your concerns to, perhaps the teacher may have some insight about yoru son to offer?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I find reassurance from your words. Its been tough for me this far we have come with my son and I want us to be close even as he growing up to become a young man. I have a good relationship with their teacher and will definitely give her a friendly call to know more from her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh good.   And it may be just a normal part of his development.   Teens pull away from parents, peer group becomes far more important.  It may be as simple as that. It took my daughter about 15 years to "come back to me" and now we have a close relationship again....so even if he seems to be drifting away know that it's normal and that will change in the future.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Good to know that from an experienced parent. I will try communicate my worries and my intensions to him. I hope he will understand and not pull away.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And in the meantime remember to take extra good care of yourself :)   Sometimes as moms we forget to do special things for ourselves.   You are doing the hardest and most imporant job in the world so it's important to recognize that and reward yourself for it.   I hope that conversation goes well for you, that you will have with him.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for your time and experienced advise.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh you're so welcome, it's been a pleasure speaking with you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: The pleasure was all mine. Bye.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: bye :)

situation: I have a friend who knew me since I was little, she is closer to my mother's age so she is kinda like my aunt growing up. She always treat me like I was little. I understand that she knew me since I was little. But I'm a grown woman now and I want our relationship to go far and grow more. I tried to explain it to her but she is not getting it. It's made me feel sad that we can't move our relationship further.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how are you feeling?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm feeling pretty good right now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything you'd like to talk to me about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have a friend who knew me since I was little. She is a little older than me. She still treat me like I'm still little. I tried to explain to her that I'm a grown woman and I grew out of lot of things when I was a kid.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is she listening to you? I know how it feels to be patronised by people who are older.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: She still didn't get it. I want our relationship to move further but with her like this. I feel sad. Is there anything I can do?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, if she is not listening to you when you try to talk to her you have to ask yourself whether the relationship is really worth it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's true. I didn't think about it. Should I approach her and talk about it before?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I think it's always worth fighting for a friendship but there does come a point when it stops being at all viable.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, that's definitely true. I know she is a good person. So I would give her a chance and we can talk about it before.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Does she have younger siblings? I am the eldest of four and I sometimes, even at age forty five, find myself trying to tell them what to do

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: She have a younger brother that is 9 years younger. She used to be only child for a while before her brother was born.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It could be the cause of her behaviour.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Maybe that's why. She have different personality than me. She is more into logical thinking and she love people and has bubbly personality. While I'm emotional but are social butterfly and after a while, I would need a break from people.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How much would it hurt you to end  the friendship? Do you think you could make other friends and survive it or is it worth fighting for?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: To be honest, there are some times when she made me cry or mad when I get home. I think that over the time and the more older I get, the more I would knowledge my relationship with her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know that I don't know her but to be honest it does sound to me like your friendship is a bit toxic.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do have other friends that I could purse the relationship farther. I think I just felt bad that I known her for years and she is a good person. It's just not what I need right now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sometimes we really just have to let things go. I know it's hard but it will probably good for your mental health.  I hope that I have been able to help you today! It's a difficult situation for sure.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, thank you! You certainly helped me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have a great rest of day :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too! Stay safe and healthy!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: (can you press quit from your end; I am not allowed to do it from mine. Cheers!)

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can completely understand this, as I was also extremely worried about money when the pandemic first started and about not being able to be employed.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's so hard to find something else right now.

dialog.content: Have you looked into hiring agencies to help you find a more permanent job during the pandemic? Hiring agencies are a great resource to find a job that fist you best.

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.content: It definitely does help to make sure you are following up!

dialog.content: When is a decent amount of time to follow up?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I usually wait a week before I reach out.

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Not a problem! I know this is an incredibly difficult situation, but I know you can make it through!

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes it has!

dialog.speaker: seeker

You've been helpful and I think we've hit our minimum that we need.
 Unless there is anything else from you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am all set! Feel free to chat back anytime you need anything!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you so much! I hope you have a good night.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too!

situation: My boyfriend broke up with me and he had control of all our expenses and now im broke and dont have a place to stay so I feel really depressed

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Im not doing very good

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm so sorry to hear that, but I would love to help. What is going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My boyfriend broke up with me because we kept fighting and now I have no money and no place to stay

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I am so sorry to hear that. breakups are definitely hard to go through, especially in the times we are in right now.
  How long were you guys together? And you were living together?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: We were together for almost 5 years and we were living together he had all the money in his name and I have nothing now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh I see that was a very long relationship! I actually just got out of a 3 1/2 year one myself, so I understand how hard it is. Are you still currently staying with him until you find your own place? Or are you staying somewhere else?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I am currently trying to find a way to get to my mom who lives about 150 miles away. Im staying with my friend for now but she said I can only stay for 2 more days
 Yeah break ups are the worst

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm glad to hear that you are with a friend right now during this hard time, and that you have a place to go! But I see the struggle is finding a way to get there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah she is a good friend im happy that I can stay with her for the time being its just stressful trying to get to my mom

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If you are struggling on finding a way back to your moms house, it might be a good idea to loan or borrow some money from either friends or family, or open a credit card just for the time being. Then once you get back to your mom's, you can start focusing on yourself and get yourself back on your feet! IT's important to not overwhelm yourself right now, so focus on one thing at a time

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah I definitely agree. I am waiting to hear back from capitalone about getting approved for a credit card and my friend said she can loan me 70$ but the bus ticket to my mom is 150$ so I'm almost to my goal I'm working on it though.
 It does make me feel better to talk about it though and lay it all out it makes it easier to figure my stuff out

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
That is great to hear! I know that you are probably also hurting from the breakup itself too, but I'm glad to hear that you are focusing on getting yourself to a safe place first. It's going to take a long time to fully heal again, but as long as you make sure you are stable, you will be able to get through this
 It also sounds like you know what you need to do, and you aren't letting yourself forget what is important. That is wonderful

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah thanks I appreciate that. I feel a lot better I hope you have an awesome day!
 I do understand what I need to do for sure 
 It was just overwhelming thinking about everything and talking it and listing it out makes me feel better for sure

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah that's great. I have confidence that you will get over this bump in your life, and good things will come to you. Keep focusing on what you need to do! You are doing great!

situation: I am at my first semester in a doctoral program and have a lot to do at once and have been feeling very anxious in the past few weeks.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good afternoon!  What can I help you with today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've been feeling a lot of pressure and stress lately due to school

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oof, yes, school can be a tough situation.   Are you close to the end of the semester?   That can be both a relief and stressful, because a break is near, but all the exams and projects are due.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I am a little more than halfway done with the semester The thing is that I am in a doctoral program, so even though the semester is close to the end, I always need to continue working

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ahh, I see.  That is a great deal of time and work.  What are you studying?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It is... I am in psychology
 I have to work on my thesis on the side even with all of my classes and I have a hard time managing my time and keeping track

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Time management can be very difficult with a normal class load, that sounds exhausting.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do calendars help you at all?  I am a super visual person, so calendars and planners are invaluable to me, as well as different colors of ink and labels so I can visually divide my attention Getting a million different colors of pens and highlighters changed my organization for the better.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do keep a physical and electronic agenda, but I feel like even if I write down my tasks, I find a way to procrastinate which is what I am having trouble with

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Also, setting clear boundaries for your study/work and relax times may help.  I like to set a timer for, say, 45 minutes, and work/write/read/study for that long, then, I get a 10 minute break.  I'll go outside, refresh my coffee, grab a snack, dance to some music, stretch, then set another timer for 45 minutes, then another break for 10 minutes.  That allows my brain to take a second and refresh from the work.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
That is actually very helpful, I'll take note of it
 I think it would help to fix break times so I can actually focus on working

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Or, like, today, I woke up and spent half an hour allowing myself to drink my coffee and scroll through Reddit.  Then it was time to work on my group project for 45 minutes, then a break, then I worked again, took a break, went to a class, etc.  Scheduling your breaks can be helpful too so your lizard brain doesn't freak out and go "WE ARE GOING TO BE WORKING FOREVERRRR"

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I agree!!! That really is helpful, thank you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: which, for me at least, is where the fatigue sets in.  You're very welcome!  I hope I was helpful.  You seem positive and willing to try new things, and I think you will be successful even if you are tired.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
The pandemic hasn't really helped either
 I'm always locked in my apartment and staying sitting down in front of my computer all day

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Definitely not.  Everyone's Maslow hierarchy getting knocked out from under them is extremely disconcerting.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hahaha, Maslow, i like it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Like how is a person supposed to study, self-actualize, when we literally don't know what will happen next?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I agree! I've also been turning to drugs a lot lately due to the situation. Procrastinating makes me feel guilty, and I end my day too early to smoke and focus my mind on something other than studying....

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are there aspects of your study that you dislike, or are you just ready to be done with school?  Little of column A + column b?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hm, I think it has less to do with liking or disliking, and more with starting a very hard and lengthy degree online without any social contact

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah, yes, that makes sense.  And working from home is infamously hard, again, for lack of boundaries, so in addition to just not seeing people and getting the casual affirmation and confirmation messages on a daily basis of like "hey Jim you did a great job on that spreadsheet" or "Haha Jessica you're so funny", we also are writing papers in our pajamas and bedclothes, which makes it seem interminable.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Ugh exactly

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Listening to lectures while the doorbell is ringing and it's just so strange and we aren't set up to do it.  That's why I'm a big fan of specific spaces in my house since March.  I tried to sit in bed with my laptop and do things but 1. I'm old and it hurts my wrists and 2. my headspace is so completely different from being my computer chair and at my desk.  I try to be really strict about where I do my work., maybe trying to structure that a bit could help you feel a little more in control of the work load and your day's schedule.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I agree, that makes a lot of sense! Thank you 
 You have been a big help today and I really feel listened and understood. I really appreciate it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am very glad to hear that!  You sound motivated to succeed and I think that you deserve to take care of yourself and stay healthy to complete your studies.  It will be hard but it will also be worth it, and you should be proud.  Good luck with everything!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Wow, thank you! 
 Have a great day

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you!  You too!
