dialog.content: i dont have anyone and i live alone. i feel that my scholarship will be taken away if i fail

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know that can be very difficult and I am proud of you reaching out for help and support! You are making great strides in talking about your feelings! A scholarship would reflect that you are very dedicated to school and that you are deserving of this award. You have a lot to be proud of, having attained a scholarship. I also had one and it can feel like a lot of pressure though.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: should i go to my school councellor

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, that is a great idea. Have you ever joined a study group? A group of peers can also be helpful.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have been really upset as people from my study group dont respond to my emails

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that and know that could make you really sad. Do you have a relationship with a professor at your school? One that you can talk to about your feelings?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I dont have a professor i can trust or feel comfortable taking to I also dont have any family  i have general anxiety disorder

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay, I can understand your feelings. Have you heard of practice tests?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i couldnt study because I have chronic pain issues

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have experienced a little bit of what you are describing and I am so happy that you have reached out regardless of your obstacles. Have you ever tried to write down what you're feeling?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i have never tries writing down my feelings

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it can help release feelings and help ground people. Do you have family that has experienced what you are feeling or been through college o a scholarship? I would suggest reaching out to family in the case of feeling anxiety.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i dont have a lot of family. I am new to this country as wel. due to covid i cant fly back home.

situation: They want me to work on a project with them. I agreed to it. Wondering how it will go.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hi

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good evening, how are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm good

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What can I help you out with tonight?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm a little nervous about accepting the project from my friend

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like a complicated situation

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: it is

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is the project something you will become responsible for?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: to a degree

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is that a responsibility you want to have, or is it a burden that is being thrust upon you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: both

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, I think if it's truly a responsibility you want, you will work to live up to it as best you can.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will. It's the best thing for me to do at the moment. I'm very much needed

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's excellent, not many people can say that!  It's starting to sound more and more like an opportunity for you to prove yourself :-)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It will be to say the least. I might have to deal with people I don't like and who don't like me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's an unfortunate part of most group projects, I hate to say.  Interpersonal communication can be so hard.  I have been a project manager in the past, and found that you have to consider Person XYZ did not wake up in the morning and decide to make your life harder.   And as long as you can keep things polite and professional, everyone gets to go home separately, thankfully!  Haha

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's a good point! lol

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Foster the people around you that can provide you support, be a reliable producer and leader, and you will find that those that don't like you will move on.  And, most importantly, you can only control how you respond/behave.  If someone wants to instigate conflict, that will reflect poorly upon them.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: So avoid them if they get hostile with me?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You can only engage with what abuse and hostility you accept.  If you smile in response, it will make you feel better than if you internalize their aggression.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: True.

situation: My boyfriend cheated in me and then broke up with me after I told him that I knew what he did.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am having a deep sense of sadness after the break up with my boyfriend. I feel like crying all the time and I feel like the sadness will never end. I have sense of overwhelming sadness as if my heart is sinking or like I am on a sinking ship. I wake up in the middle of the night and I am gasping for air, too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thanks for sharing that with me. I'm so sorry you've been feeling like that. Breakups are so hard and can cause so much grief.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I feel like I will never meet anyone else and that I wasted so much time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know those feelings of sadness can feel like they will never end- I have felt that way too before.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Do you feel like the best part of your life was over? That is how I feel now. Its seem like all of my plans for the future are over and ruined.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: But they do pass eventually and become easier to manage- with time and care. Do you have anyone in your life, a friend or family member who you can lean on when you're feeling so sad?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have a best friend but she told me that she didn't like my boyfriend from the beginning so she is glad that we broke up. Well, that I got dumped.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like it's hard to have your best friend not be as supportive as you want during this hard time. What has been helping you to pass the time these days- do you have any activities or hobbies that you like to do to relax, have fun, or take your mind of things?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have been wanting to buy a bike and start taking my dog out for little bike rides. I want to get one with a basket and have her sit in the front. I just hate being alone and I feel so sad about doing everything alone now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's a great idea. I love taking walks and bike rides outside when I feel down. I also have a little dog and she helps me whenever I'm feeling lonely- dogs are great at providing comfort and unconditional love.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I just bought my puppy and she is super comforting. I think that because she was a recuse dog I feel a greater attachment. It is kind of nice having her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: My dog has also helped me meet other people and make friends- sometimes I'll walk my dog with others or go on bike rides with friends. If there is a dog park or local park nearby, maybe you can go and talk to other people there- talking about your dogs is a great icebreaker!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know about a local dog park and think I will visit it today. That is a really good idea. I don't need a bike for that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm glad to hear that! I hope you have a great time there! Research has also shown that spending time outside everyday is really good for our mental health.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You are giving me something to think about that isn't related to the break up. I feel like a weight is sort of lifting off of me. I think I needed to talk and get some of my feelings on the table today.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If you are still interested in getting a bike, I'd say go for it too! Biking is a great stress relief and form of exercise that often makes me feel better.  I am so glad to hear that and am so happy you are talking to me is making you feel better.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I really want to get one but since I don't have a lot of money right now I will waitr on that and go to the dog park instead. I also heard about yoga iin the park.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Those are both great ideas! Yoga in the park sounds awesome. Yoga is another great stress reliever and form of exercise that is often free or very inexpensive.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am feeling like life still goes on and things are going on all around me. I am going to start seeing whats out there and trying to join in a little bit more. My dog is opening a door for me and with your encouragement too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am very glad to hear that. Is there anything else I can help you with today? I am here to listen.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I was thinking about reading self help books or journaling but I was so down on myself.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Reading and journaling are also great tools to use. Even when you are feeling down it can be helpful to write down your feelings as a form of release.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for all of your support and understanding today. I am going to journal and then head to the dog park and get some fresh air.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like a great plan. Good luck and I hope you continue to feel better.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you again for being a wonderful listener and better friend than my best one.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're very welcome.

situation: My coworker is constantly telling me what to do like she is my boss. I told her about it and she still does it. I cannot make a move without criticism or a comment on how to do it her way. I how to do my job and it angers me when she expects me to do it her way.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am feeling angry today about work.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh no, what happened to make you feel this way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My coworker expects me to do things the way she does and is constantly critical. I just want her to understand that I am able to do things my way and it is still okay. I told her this and she ignored me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sure that's terribly frustrating having someone treat you like that. Is this something that your boss is aware of?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have not told her yet. I do not want to get her into trouble and I want to have a good relationship with her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can you discuss it with her in a respectful way to get her to understand that you're not trying to undermine her but that this is how you work most efficiently? And maybe mention that you don't want to involve the boss?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's something I could try. I really care about my coworker and it makes me so angry that she treats me this way.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think sometimes it's easy to jump to anger when you feel hurt. It might be worth telling her how you feel and also maybe ask why she thinks you should be doing things her way. Maybe there's a give and take in this situation. Some of her methods could be beneficial.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Do you have any advice on how to start my conversation with her? I have found some of her ideas to be beneficial. I just would like my ideas expressed as well. I feel we can learn from each other.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Tell her the things that you like about her method and maybe suggest some of your methods to her as well. Maybe she'll find them to be more efficient. Make it a partnership rather than a fight for whose method is better.
 She could feel threatened by you so it's worth digging into those feelings with her and proving to her that you're working toward the same goal.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's a great idea. It is possible. I am the new kid on the block. I will try by being non confrontational. Any specific ideas to how  I can do that? She gets offended easily. I want her to know I am helping.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'd suggest how much she's helped you since you are in fact the new kid on the block.  And tell her that you want to work with her to have the most efficient partnership possible and use each of your talents to the best of your ability. Then you can lay out how you work best and see where it goes from there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks! I will try that. You made me feel so much better!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm so glad, these can be frustrating situations but sometimes it's worth looking at the other person's perspective.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I agree. Sometimes you just have to take a step back and have a conversation with someone not in the thick of it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Absolutely, I have no emotional stake in this. The two of you do and that can cloud things sometimes.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think people get territorial when they have worked somewhere and a new person comes in. I see that now. I am optimistic we will work it out and be a great team!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great to hear, I'm glad we were able to chat!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Me too! You really helped me. Have a wonderful day! I know I will since you helped me out.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great, good luck!

situation: I wish I had a girlfriend. I haven't seen any friends outside of my household since last October!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello. How are you? What would you like to talk about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Got any advice for a lonely single fellow in his mid thirties? I miss my friends, and virtual meetings on Zoom don't feel the same as meeting in person!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: The pandemic has been hard on many people, those in relationships and those looking for new ones! Have you tried to develop a new hobby or interest that you can do with others once the pandemic is over?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, I've been writing enough poetry to publish a book, so I'll certainly do that. I know the pandemic has been rough for everyone. Hopefully my doctor will say I qualify for the vaccine soon.  Getting my stimulus check in the next week or so would certainly make me a bit happier, too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great! Poetry is something you can share in person or over video link. What about physical activities? I think that yoga might be something that you can do by yourself and with others. There are a bunch of videos on sites like YouTube.  A stimulus check! Yes, we can all use money during these trying times.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh, I took advantage of virtual yoga and zumba early on during the pandemic. Friends had virtual classes. Eventually they were able to go back to work. Being disabled is a poverty trap for a lot of people, not just me. I really want to find a girlfriend once I get vaccinated.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have tried yoga myself, but it wasn't really my thing. I prefer walking and riding a bike.  Do you have to wait until you get vaccinated to start working on getting a girlfriend?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, certain things don't work for every person.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: True. I like trying new things, but I am happy to give them up if they just don't fit my style.  Is there a way you could start looking for a girlfriend online now, before the pandemic is over? Maybe there is a way to make a connection using your poetry.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Technically, no, I don't HAVE to wait to get vaccinated, but then again, maybe I don't want a girlfriend. The only one I had ten years ago in college wanted to marry me and have a child and I wasn't ready for commitment. I fear marriage, actually.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Marriage is a big step, and any prudent person would think carefully before making the commitment.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's a good point, I'm sure if my poetry is entertaining or full of truth about my experiences in a wheelchair, perhaps a woman will find that attractive

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you concerned that things will move too quickly to that level of commitment (marriage)? You could make it clear from the outset that you want to take it slow.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Or I could just get an adult entertainer to be my companion for a day. I've done that before with women in that industry. It costs a bit, but it's worth it for companionship 
 No, I just hate the idea of waking up to one woman for 20 or 30 years
 But it's better than being alone

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Robin Williams talked about that situation. He said that he didn't fear being alone as much as being with people that he didn't like.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Did he really? Well, it's sad how he ended up, I'm too scared to commit suicide like he did! I enjoy living life, even when I have months at a time without human connection

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Adult entertainers provide one kind of companionship, but it is not complete. It feels like a stopgap to me. Is it fulfilling enough for you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You're right, I just lack self confidence in my poetry....hopefully I'll figure out a way to spit it out on a blog or something

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You could trade poems with other budding poets. "I'll show you mine if you show me yours." Do you find yourself to be more an introvert or an extrovert?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh it's been very fulfilling for me when I've had the opportunity to use it. Most of them are genuine women. Only a handful have tried to take advantage of me.  I'm definitely an extrovert That was a clever pun by the way  It's ironic and strange how many people struggle with that stupid voice in their head that tells them they're not good enough to accomplish anything.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thanks. Ok. The pandemic is particularly hard on extroverts. People look down on sex workers, but I think that the world is a big complex place and people are trying to make their way in it. If everyone is consenting, I see no problem.  Have you tried psilocybin? Some people swear that it changes the inner monologue for the better. I am not a physician, but for long-term adjustment, it might be beneficial.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well of course it's consentual. I would never NOT ask for permission to do things with a sex worker, or any person.  I'll have to Google that, I've never heard of that, but thanks for the advice!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sure! I'm glad we had a chance to chat. Best of luck with making friends/girlfriends after the pandemic! BTW, is there a quit button on your end?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes there is, and I am pressing it right now. Thanks so much, I'll be fine. Have a good night!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good night!

situation: I was striving for a job after doing bachelors and then covid-19 happened and things did not go like I planned. Now i am jobless and its huge Concern for me and made me feel like a useless person most of the time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Hello
 How you doing today?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How may I help you? I am doing good ... thanks..

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: well now a days I am struggling to keep myself sane because of not having a job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ohh.. you currently in between jobs. Is that correct?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: well I am not doing any job just freelancing which is a stable and guarantee income

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you looking for job?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I am looking for one but so far not succeeded at all.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How did you prepare your resume? Have you had it revieed by any professional resume writer?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I made my resume from a website called Novoresume and time to time I update my resume too. It looks pretty professional.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: When you submit resume, first it is scanned by a software for key words. Are you sure you have all the right key words, so that the scanning software picks it up?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What are the keywords? do you know about anything it? I do not email the PDF file but the word file so my resume do not get ignore by the software company is using. Do you think Covid-19 has anything to do with it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If you don't have the right key words, it may not be picked up. The professional resume writers typically run the resume through the scanning software and make sure it is picked up at least 90% of the time. For some businesses, COVID might be an issue.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay but what are the keywords? Is it different for different business?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yes, you need to compile keywords... from the potential job postings. ..and add them to your resume somehow...

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Covid-19 cause problem for everyone I guess not only me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: that's for sure..

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay but I do not think so keywords are the issue.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you getting call from the employers?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am not getting calls from HR deptartment.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: good.. then keyword is not the issue..

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Covid situation is getting better now so maybe I get a call.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you getting interview call? I hope thigs will work out for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah before covid I got two calls but during the Covid period not even one. Yeah I hope so.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think the situation will get better soon...

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah I hope so.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Anything else I could help you with?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No that was helpful.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: thanks.. have a good day.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too.

situation: With everything going on, my depression has taking a turn for the worst.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how can I help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: This pandemic is causing me a lot of anxiety and stress.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything that's specifically causing the stress? Probably some big changes?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My job safety, financial situation, get evicted and much more.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you're facing some potential instability which can definitely increase anxiety. What strategies have you been trying so far?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: None so far. I have no idea. Any suggestions?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: For the short term, have you tried looking in to meditation or breathing exercises? For a longer approach, have you thought about gaining some new skills for the job market?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh ok. That is helpful. I've been reading to past the time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sounds like reading might be a good way to get your mind out of the anxiety loop, does that sound right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, sometimes.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great! Maybe you can take that a step further and read some material on mindfulness

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Not my type of books, but I'll definitely look into it. 
 Job stability is also a concern for me
 I definitely fear for my future if I lose my job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So, you don't feel like you have a secure spot right now, is that right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is correct.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can you tell me what your job is?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I work for an insurance company. Low level employee.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ok, have there been any talks about layoffs? Or any concerns about your performance?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, but due to this pandemic, layoff is a concern.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you're nervous about things that might happen in the future, but there's no talks about anything concrete?

situation: Removed from my job - apparently only because of my age - no longer doing meaningful work.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not too bad, really. Just kinda pissed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sorry to hear you're upset. Want to share why you're feeling that way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Sure, I guess "venting" is pretty much all I have that I can do at this point.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you feel like you can't take any actions in the situation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Long story short - at work, a new boss pulled all the leadership OUT of the jobs they were hired for (some more than 12 years ago - so they have lots of experience) and shuffled them all around to new jobs - all that is except the 5 oldest employees - they got moved into "non-positions". No longer supervising anyone, only doing grunt work, etc. It ticks me off and there's nothing I can do about it.
 I'm working on a *possible* action - RETIREMENT from this job. Only problem is - bad time to be doing that; pressure to keep my income up until my daughter graduates college, etc.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds frustrating, especially if you're one of the employees affected and when the new boss doesn't seem to know what he/she is doing with the reorganization. That's positive, though, that you at least have a strategy in mind with retirement, even if this may not be the best time right now.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
She has a "plan", I guess, but it isn't being articulated that well, and these actions are demonstrating poor "people management" and seem to be both confusing lots of folks, and professionally insulting many others. Oh, and by the way, things are running WORSE than they were before, but it's being blamed on COVID
 Problem with the "retirement strategy" is that it means going out on a limb with no job with the holidays coming up, and with COVID with no end in sight. SO, just "muddling through" the days these days, hoping better times are ahead.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you are not the only worker who is seriously questioning the changes being made.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh, no. 1 person outright quit immediately. 1 other has retired already. 1 more I know is retiring by New Years. Others are more "stuck", as they just CAN'T afford to quit OR retire. So, yeah, us "old folk" are not "happy campers"

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow! From what you've said, it sounds like your coworkers feel much as you do about the changes.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yep. We are sort of our own "support group", but since we are all 100% teleworking these days, we don't "see" each other, though we try to touch base through phone calls at least once a week.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That must be tough, but it must help to at least commiserate through the phone calls and be able to relate to each other.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yep. Best I can hope for at this point. If I find another job to transition to, that would improve things. Until then, gotta just "keep on keeping on"

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: My former spouse works for a company that has moved him to departments he needed to retrain to work in, and he is considering looking for a different job, maybe after COVID is outta here. It sounds like you're determined to stay as positive as possible while considering other options, such as a new job. Doing something about it like this must feel a little better.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: As good as it can. Thanks for your time and commiseration.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome! I also think that's wise to hold off on retirement until your daughter is through college. My oldest is ready for college and I don't feel ready to leave work, either.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yep, and she's actually a senior, so she'll graduate in May. But I gotta keep my motivation up until then. ;)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You can do it!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks! I guess we'll see. Have a great evening.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too! G'night.

situation: The pandemic is causing my company to close. I am now going to be searching for a job in a saturated market!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm so anxious. I'm losing my job at the end of the month and I don't know how my resume is going to hold up with so many other people looking for work too!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand why you would feel this way. Maybe you should try to take a step back and reassess the situation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Maybe, but it's hard to feel like there is another way to approach it. There's so many people struggling. It's all so sad and scary right now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It may be a good time to go over your resume, making sure to fine tune all of your achievements and qualifications. This may help ease some of your worries and fears.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Wow, that's a great idea. 
 Hmm. But what if I spend the fee to get my resume looked at  but then don't land a job? Wouldn't it be better to save the money and hedge my bets?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It seems that you are mixed up in the decision to put in extra funds in a professional resume polish or use your time and energy instead of actual financial means. Is that right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Exactly. I'm anxious that if I don't get professional help I won't land a job but then on the other hand I'm anxious that if I don't get a job I'll have needed the savings.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Perhaps you should speak to a trusted mentor or a closed loved one and seek their opinion. Often, we need someone outside of the actual situation that can give us great advice.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's a good idea too. Maybe even someone who recently changed jobs can give me some interview tips! -Even if I get the interview I know I'm going to be anxious about that!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand we can all feel anxious during an interview, but remember what great qualities you can bring to the table and believe in yourself!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'll try! Thank you so much for the advice!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Also, there are many online resources that are great at finding available jobs and require your time and effort, but not any money invested. This might be a good tool as well!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's very true. Maybe I'll get started on that tonight.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hope so. I've also been in a similar situation so I can completely understand your feelings. Keep in mind there are also resume sites that are free to use, as I know you are concerned about the financial part in that area.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's nice to hear! Sometimes it feels like I'm being dramatic. I really appreciate your thoughts, having an action plan really helps me feel less anxious. Thank you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can I help with anything else?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't think so. I already feel excited to get started looking at those resources and tweaking my achievements.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Your feelings are completely valid and I wish you the best. You got this!

situation: I recently broke up with my girlfriend. I have been feeling very lonely and depressed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hi

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello, I am struggling a lot right now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm here for you. what would you like to talk about

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's about my recent breakup. My girlfriend left me last week. I honestly don't think I can handle the loneliness.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's a hard situation to go through. How long were you together

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: We were together for almost 2 years

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If you don't mind me asking. how old are you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am 24 years old.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ok so you were together with this person for ten percent of your life almost. that is a significant relationship

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It surely is. I definetely feel somewhat worthless now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is understandable. would you like to tell me what you plan on doing

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Sometimes I have suicidal feelings...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a serious emotion. Have you been talking to anyone yet

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, I live alone

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you have family or friends you can reach out too

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have one friend who lives in my neighborhood, but I dont trust him much.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you considered calling a help line. They are free, private and very professional

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel like those are useless to be honest

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. Would you happen to be in college?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I am at my last year of college right now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you tried to reach out to any of the free student services such as councillors.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, althought I prefer those than help lines.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
your situation is unique to you and they would be there to help 
 That what you pay tuition for.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What if they make fun of me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That would get them fired.  but it's ok if you don't want to go to them. I would suggest talking to your friend first at least. Even if you don't trust him. You need to express your emotions to someone.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I honestly don't feel like expressing my emotions would be of any help. Do they even care lol?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am here to listen. please express your emotions

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I just want to kill myself already...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would no suggest that. Your life is important.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: She was such an important part of my life... I can't live on without her. She was the only thing that I actually cared for. How can you say my life is important without her?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. She was important. but now she is gone. You can accept this , or not. I would suggest accepting is the better option.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well I think I rather not accept it and just end my life. Seems easier.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes I agree it would be easier. Suddenly the pain will end, because you will end.

situation: Not having enough money to pay my bills.

dialog.content: Just financial problems

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There's a lot of that going on these days, it can be rough. I know how you feel. Do you have any plans you want to put into play to solve the problem?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I'm attempting to sue money and pay off my debts and hopefully everything will get sorted out
 save*

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great to hear. Do you have anyone close to you that can help you out in a bind?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My mom has helped loan me money to pay bills that are about to be cut off. Just a small bit though. It has helped a lot though

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's excellent! It's always great to have someone you can depend on. I feel like that gave you some relief.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It has for sure. Another thing that's bothered me is my dad passed away 3-almost 4 years ago. He was my rock and would help me out in any situation. 
 So in hard times like I'm in right now, if he was still here I know he would help me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh I'm so sorry to hear that. I bet that makes things tougher. I have no doubt you'll overcome these hurdles in due time. Nothing lasts forever, that includes bad times.

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you working right now? And if so is the job secure and pay pretty well?

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, that's another reason Im on MTurk. Trying to make some extra! I wish I could find a part time job working from home.
 I did work for the Census but its ended

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well I have no doubt everything will work out great for you, just keep your head up. It's been so nice talking to you, I hope I've been of some help.

dialog.speaker: seeker

situation: I've always struggled with depression. I just feel hopeless.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm a bit bad honestly.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can you tell me what's going on to cause you to feel this way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've been struggling with depression for a while. It's hard to deal with. I feel hopeless.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I see. I definitely understand how long bouts of depression can make you feel more hopeless. Have you discussed your depression with anyone else before?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I've spoken to a therapist but it didn't help. I tried a help hotline online but they thought I was suicidal so they called the cops to check on me .. never doing that again lol. 
 I don't know what to do

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah, it sounds like you aren't suicidal. Though I do have to check; have you had any thoughts or tendencies of suicide or self-harm?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I have in the past. Recently, no. 
 I just feel like I'll never be happy.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. Can you provide any more information about your meeting with the therapist? You said talking to one didn't help, do you know why?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I think it didn't help because I'm a bit shy. Or because I didn't know where to start. It's hard to know what to talk about.
 I guess it also didn't help because she seemed like she was just reading a script. She wasn't the greatest at making me feel cared about
 You're doing pretty good though haha

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That makes sense and is completely natural. It's difficult sometimes to open up to a stranger that you don't completely know or might not trust. It definitely sounds frustrating that she seemed like she was reading from a script.  And I'm glad to hear you think I'm doing alright lol

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think therapy could work. Actually something happened with my therapist (maybe fired lol) and she's no longer my therapist.. so I need a new one.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's interesting. I know from experience that finding a good therapist is all about trial and error, unfortunately. It's important to find the right fit and I would definitely suggest looking for a new one. Do you know of anyone in your life, maybe a friend or family member, that has been to therapy that you would be comfortable getting a recommendation from?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think my closest friend has been to therapy. I could always ask her for a recommendation.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like a great idea! Remember to keep your friends close too. I know it can be tough when you feel as low as this, but it's important to have a good support system in addition to any therapy you might be receiving.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My friends and I look out for eachother. I think they're a good help :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great to hear! It really sounds like you can go to them for support and often being there for someone else can make us feel better too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: This was a nice little chat! You should actually volunteer for a helpline haha

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Aww that's so good of you to say!! I do want to confirm that you have a game-plan now, though. Remember to keep your friends close and to start looking for a new therapist if you haven't already. If you find one that's not the right fit it doesn't hurt to start over or maybe even ask for a recommendation from that therapist. If they're any good they should understand how important it is for you to find a good fit. Also remember to be kind to yourself. It can be difficult, but I know you can do it.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I'm ok! I got my friends and family. I made an appointment with my psychiatrist to get different medication so I could ask him for a therapist recommen
dation as well.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's absolutely wonderful to hear! Is there anything else you would like to discuss today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I'm good. Thank you though! This was nice :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Awesome. You've got this! Have a good rest of your day

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too! :)

situation: It started during my childhood, when I was 9 years old, living my father. He had a bad temper and was impatient with me, especially with school. He would raise his voice at me and get physical when I didn't do well in classes. After the age of 10, I ended up living with my mother but I would spend most of my time with my friends because I enjoyed their company more than anything. I started to hate dealing with school because I felt I wouldn't be able to do well in school; I started to get anxiety from dealing with tests and it gotten bad to the point where I couldn't really stay in crowded place as well. Today, I still try my best to tolerate going to college and do my best in classes even when my anxiety comes back.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, my name is Carolyn.  How are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello, my name is Nicholas. I am feeling a bit tired today, how are you Carolyn?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm a bit tired too to be honest!   Is there anything you'd like to talk about today?  I'm ready to listen :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have been tired a lot lately, even when I sleep for 8 hours. I am not sure what to really do to help myself gain more energy and recover my sleeping schedule to normal.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I see.  Are you finding it hard to function on not enough sleep?  I have experienced that at times as well, and found it quite frustrating! How much sleep are you getting now?   Do you keep irregular sleep hours, e.g. the time you go to bed and get up?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, I had a bad sleeping schedule, when I would go to sleep around 2-3am. I now get around 8 hours of sleep but I feel like it isn't enough still.
 Would this relate to a health problem of not being active or maybe stress?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I used to be a night owl, going to bed early in the morning.  I reset my schedule very gradually.  I went to bed 15 minutes earlier, and set an alarm for 8 hours later.  I did that for 3 days, then I would ratchet it back another 15 minutes.   It was a gradual process and the only way I could fix it. Are you getting any exercise during the daytime?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I see, I should set more alarms for myself. I have not put the time to exorcise as I would like to. I was planning on ordering a pair of weighted jump ropes for myself.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Even a walk may help you sleep...I know it does for me,   Jump ropes are a great idea!   I was advised it's better to exercise earlier in the day, not too close to bedtime or it can affect sleep. Do you have trouble getting to sleep or waking up in the night?   Or is it more a case of just not feeling rested after your sleep?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I mostly work and go to school during the day so there really isn't too much time unless I decide to exorcise early in the morning. By the end of the day, I already feel exhausted from work and most of my motivation to exorcise is gone. 
 I sometimes wake up in the middle of the night.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I see.   Sleep problems are very common you certainly aren't alone in this.  With some changes I am confidant you can improve your sleep.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Do you have any suggestions other than exorcising to help with sleeping?
 Anything would be taken into consideration if possible.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I listen to a really good podcast called Tracks to Relax.  It's guided meditation that helps me deeply relax before I sleep. sometimes I use it in the middle of the night too, if I can't fall back asleep! Have you tried meditation before bed?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh wow, I never really thought listening to relaxing music to help go back to sleep. I will try that out tonight! I have never done meditation before bed, mostly during the day.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is your room dark?  Light blocking curtains can help.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Its decently covered and at night it is pretty dark.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And a small snack of carbs, like a peice of toast or some cereal, before bed can help too.   As you can see I have tried many things myself as I too have had sleep issues.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will try that out as well if listening to relaxing sounds doesn't help. Thank you very much for responding to my questions and needs!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I certainly know how feeling tired can impact one's life, I have found it frustrating when I have a lot to do and just don't have the energy. You are very welcome, it's been a pleasure talking with you :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Likewise, Thank you again! I hope you have a wonderful day/evening!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Take care Nicholas!  You as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too as well Carolyn!

situation: My business is suffering because of the pandemic. I'm very worried I won't be able to pay my bills soon.

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: supporter
