dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: maybe you could distritubute samples to hospitals for the nurses as a christmas present. put your card in. you KNOW they have job stability and it could lead to sales!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's something to think about.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: if you have a facebook page you can always make a post saying you are giving back to the community by giving the essential workers chrismas gifts.... it could make people in your community want to support you back!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah that's true too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i think you will be fine. you are resourceful, you are supplementing income with mturk, thats awesome. you seem to have a good head on your shoulders

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah I think you're right.

situation: I am finding the second lockdown intensely difficult to deal with, even worse than the first.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello there, would you be able to give me some advice?  The first lockdown was bad enough but the second has really thrown me into despair. I simply cannot cope without being able to see friends and family for much longer.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would be glad to. What is troubling you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have run out of coping mechanisms!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I completely understand and had similar experiences. What were some ways you tried to interact with people last shut down?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I use facebook quite a lot and it helps but it cannot compensate for face to face contact. I really need to be able to see people.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Digital contact does have its limitations. Could you meet with people outside at a distance?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, part of the problem with that is that I am not sure what the rules are any more! We have been told so many different things that I am out of the loop.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. Try sticking to the basics to make it less stressful. Wear a mask outside at a distance.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I always do wear a mask.. I think we legally have to :). I really miss little things like going out for coffee or to the gym though. It makes me feel like will never be the same again.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have to talked about this with friends? They may be feeling the same way. Just talking this out with the may be reassuring.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have a little bit but I don't want to burden them. The other aspect is that it is making me anxious about many others things too. I keep having morbid thoughts about what will happen if the pandemic never ends.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That’s understandable. Maybe talking to a professional who you don’t have to worry about burdening with your concerns will  allow you to talk about your concerns.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's why I'm here ;). You are being very helpful, thank you! It's just nice to be able to tell people what I am worried about and not have them think that I am just being silly.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Many people are struggling with the current state of affairs. Don’t be afraid to talk to people they may also be afraid to talk to people and you could miss out on supporting each other.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That does sound like really good advice. The other thing I haven't mentioned yet is that I have been having financial pressures because I am on furlough. I am worried that if this lasts much longer I will end up deep in debt.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry that wasn’t helpful.  Have you tried looking into local recourses in your city?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I felt that you were very helpful. It is nice to just know that someone is listening. I do not think that there is much in the way of local resources at the moment - everyone is very stretched.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is true it has been hard for many people.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do feel better for having been able to talk to you about this - thank you very much!  I think I will be able to carry on for a bit now. Thanks again and best wishes!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I’m glad I could provide you some support

situation: breakup with my lover

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello how are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
not so well
 what about you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm fine.  I'm so sorry that you're not feeling well, but I'm here to help.  Could you tell me what's going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
my love is broken up
 she left me alonr

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm so sorry to hear that.  Everyone goes through break ups like that at some point in their life.  I would suggest moving on and focusing on yourself for a bit.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: but how could I, we are in a relationship for 4 years suddenly she left me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand it's difficult when you were in a relationship for so long.  But it's not going to do you any good to cling on to the past.  What was her reason for leaving you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: she is engaged to a richman and they prepared to get married soon i dont have any idea for what to do

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's even a better reason for you to just forget about her, with her cheating on you like that, you know she's not the type for you.  You're so much better than her.  So just focus on yourself.  If you need some help emotionally, find yourself a counselor to talk to.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I tried to forget her, but whenever i tried the memories are repeating again and again which sometimes makes me mad

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Just try to focus on your hobbies, and hang out with your close friends.  Keep yourself occupied on other things so you wouldn't have to think about her.  Just think that you're lucky that it ended now, and it would've been worse if you found out about the cheating even later.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
whenever i try to share my feelings with my friends, they are making fun on me
 and irritates me so much

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Awww maybe try to share you feelings with your family then

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: but its not easy to me tell it to my family members

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I see.  Over time you'll definitely be able to get over it.  There are so many other women out there that are better than her.  She may have been the perfect woman for you at one point but you really should come to terms that she isn't the one for you anymore.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will try to think like that is that really works

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes just focus on positivity, doing fun things.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will try my level best

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay that's good.  I can tell that you're making too big of a deal of the situation.  You can think about the good times you've had with her, but that's in the past now so you should be focused on the future instead.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
thankyou
 i try to do this

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm happy to provide you advice.  If you need advice in the future, you could always find a counselor for you.  I hope you are feeling a bit better.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: o think so

situation: Loneliness. Just tired of being alone and feeling isolated. This is the time of year when family gatherings happen, but I live alone.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Good afternoon. Hope you are surviving he virus

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, I hope you are as well. Is anything the matter?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am just looking for a friend to chat with. Just feeling lonely.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I feel the same way. I cant wait for life to be normal again. It has been a hard year.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: The virus has stopped people from meeting. What about you? I don't know if life will ever be normal again.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you live alone? Do you mean meeting new people or getting together with people you already know? I am naturally an introvert, so it honestly didnt bother me the first month or so... but I'm so over it now. I wish I had taken advantage of freedoms we had before all of this.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I currently live in a place that is totally lonely. It is not because of the virus but just because people aren't able to reach out or there are no viable places to meet and greet.  The freedoms are still there but the fear of getting the virus stops people from normal life.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it will pass eventually, but who knows when. It feels like every where you look things are falling apart sometimes. What kind of place do you live in?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Currently I live in a hotel getting ready to travel to another state. What about you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're so right.... I miss not fearing the virus. I don't fear at as much as I did, but I fear giving it to a loved one. Do you travel a lot for work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No for personal reasons. I just find the disparity between wearing the mask in some states is not as demanding s others. Here the masks do not seem to be mandatory, but 100 miles and it is necessary.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Some people are on lockdowns, I'm nervous about our income in the next few months with the talk of more stay at home orders. I know it feels like itll be like this forever but I really dont believe it will. It may get worse before it gets better but I think things will go back to normal eventually.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think we are in a long period of recovery because the country has too many laws in different states that are causing demands on the health system.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have traveled states recently and the difference is crazy! I went through Tennessee, missippi, and alabama and stopped a few times.. some places are just going on like normal, hardly any masks.. in arkansas you never see anyone indoors without one.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Too much politics behand the virus and helping the people. Florida is mandatory, but Georgia is lacks. Tennessee is mandatory but other states are not as caring.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I totally agree. And you never know what is true or not. Its stressful. I'm in georgia now and most people do not wear them unless asked. I've only been required to at dr appointment. Is it mandatory where you are now?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No except at WalMart Georgia has a high virus rate, yet no mandatory masks.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There are signs here but half the shoppers dont wear them. I think people here dont fear it as much as other places.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: There is no mandatory requirement for the mask, especially at the convenient stores.  You walk in and the employees don't even wear them. The screens aren't  going to stop the spread.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you travel because you want to? YES the gas station closest to me.... I have been in there every other day recently and never seen a person in a mask. The employees at the dollar general close to me dont wear them either. I dont think it will either honestly. Sometimes I feel guilty if I'm not wearing one. The uncertainty of everything gets to me.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I wear mine for my own protection. What about you? I have to travel to Virginia soon and I want to arrive clean
 I am only traveling now because of issues otherwise I would be in FLorida
 I miss the bneaches

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I wear mine to protect other people.... I do have health issues that could complicate things but I dont thing it will stop me from getting it.  I MISS THEM TOO. We were in PCB last summer and everyone was freaking about the water lol. we had no idea what was coming

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I understand for protecting others, but when the people around you ignore the virus and refuse a mask, they aren't concerned about you. What part of Florida. I was near Stuart and Jensen Beaches. Masks are mandatory otherwise no entry at the stores

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: well I hope you are able to connect with people soon.. I think these are cool.. Its nice to talk to new. Youre right generally people dont care for other people, but I'm not that way. Panama City Beach.  new people*

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I care about others, but what good is it if they ignore your health. Hey glad yo chat. Catch you later.

situation: I just lost my job of seven years and am anxious about finding a new one during the holidays in a pandemic.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello, I'm feeling a lot of anxiety.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that.  Do you know what may be causing ytour anxiety?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I found out I'm losing my job on January 1.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You lost your job? thats a tough situation. Is it due to covid?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, not due to covid. It's a gig job, much like mturk, but the company is dropping people from my state on that date.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know its tough especially with the holidays and all. You have so many great skills and are such a hard worker I dont think you will have a problem at all finding something different.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've been looking at other companies, but learning a new system after all these years is difficult.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I remember a time I lost my job and felt my whole world crumbling but then I realized when one door closes another door opens. when those doors close it seems that its usually for the best because it opens the path for something bigger and greater for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's true. I've thought about looking into new fields.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you looked into some evening classes or maybe online schooling? I hear there are so many different options when it comes to online learning.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I was looking into that last night, but with covid,  classes have had to cut enrollment and are already full for next semester.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: They are full? well what about surrounding towns? Have you sat down and really thought about your passions and what you enjoy doing? Maybe theres an elderly person who needs some companionship or something like that in town for the time being.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't have a car, so I'm stuck doing classes locally. I think I'll just have to wait for the next semester.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is waiting until next semester something your willing to do? That would give you time to reflect on yourself and your future and figure out what you want to do as a career. I could give you a list of other online opportunities to make money if you would be interested.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would be interested in that, thank you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: your welcome. There are a lot of online options out there. That may be easier for you at the time being without a car. Do you have a support system of family or friends around here?

situation: just everyday life

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: having a rough day.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry you are having a rough day. Can you share more about it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i feel like my life is an unscheduled mess. nothing ever happens when its supposed to i plan things and it always falls through and doesnt happen

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like the chaos and disorganization is really bother you.  Is that right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can certainly understand.  When my children were young and I worked full-time outside of the home, I found it very difficult to keep things in my life organized.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: my partners schedule changes about 3 times a day. so i never know if what i had planned will happen or not. usually not.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand why that may frustrate you. Does your partner have any control over their schedule and the changes?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i stay home all the time and even our plans for doing things at home fall through. he will promise to do something simple and months later its still not done its supposed to be a set schedule but something always come up and he ends up working anywhere from 10 to 12 hours

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: This can be very stressful and lead to a lot of problems in a relationship if not dealt with early.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: its more of the reasons why he has to work late that bother me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you tried hanging a large calendar and writing down his schedule as well as any plans you made? Then each morning, do a quick 1-minute check on the calendar to ensure you are BOTH on the same page. Can you tell me about the reason?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: the people he works with either screw up a machine or flake out not even working an 8 hour shift. yet my other half is always the one to stay

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is he aware of your concerns?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i mean i admire his work ethic but why does it always have to fall on him to be the one to stay we have talked about it often

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I feel like I had very similar issues in my first marriage.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: it makes my anxiety go crazy

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Communication can be very key here. It sounds like he is dedicated to his job, which can be a good thing, but I do understand that you want to be put first sometimes as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: we talk often and i have actually broken down several times and cried. funny thing is im not mad about the situation itself. i just feel robbed sometimes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand why that would make you feel that way.  We enjoy spending time with the people that we love.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: it would be different if i didnt care or it made me mad. it just make me feel alone

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Why don't you choose even one night each week, and see if he will commit to being home that one night - then plan something special TOGETHER.  It could be a game night, cooking dinner together, or just sitting talking.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: we have tried that and he still got stuck working.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Also, when he is home, you could both try REALLY connecting during that time.  Then maybe when he is at work, you won't feel so alone having made that connection that morning or the night before.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: we do make the most of our 2 to 3 hours together at night. otherwise i would completely break down

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is good. Does he understand your feelings or does he tend to dismiss them?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: he feels the same way about our time

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So it sounds like time together is important to him as well, and the fact that you are both on the same page about that is wonderful.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: we had a lot of talks about this issue

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Instead of you making the plans, why don't you try giving him suggestions and have him make the plans next time?  Perhaps by him making them, he will be more focused on ensuring that he can be there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that sound like a great idea

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And then he will also see how much effort you put in! Please let me know how it work out!  I wish you the best of luck!  Have a wonderful evening!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you for your help

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are very welcome!

situation: I really like this man. He was attractive and spoiled me. I thought it was going to work out. Then I found out from another friend he found another woman to have sex with. It hurt me a lot!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I am so embarassed and saddened.
 I can not believe he cheated on me.
 I thought we were going to work out. I was really attracted into him and I cared for him a lot.
 I wanted to date him longer.
 Thank Goodness I did not sleep with him. What a disaster.
 Why are men like this?
 Why do they break women's hearts?
 I wish there were more decent men out there that did not play around.
 Men can be such pigs!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: what happened?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I met a handsome intelligent man.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: how  are you feeling lately?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
We dated for a month.
 Then out of nowhere he cheated on me.
 I'm so sad.
 I am just trying to understand what I did wrong.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: it's okay. we should learn from things like this.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
That's true.
 It's crazy we have to experience heart ache.
 To learn...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: don't think much and keep yourself engaged in the activities you like the most.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hey, that is great advice!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: can you tell me how are you before this incident?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I was happy.
 But when a man breaks your heart, it makes you feel very ugly.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: can you share me some happy memories which made you smile can you share me some happy memories which made you smile

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Sure, I love going to the movies! How about you!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: it's okay. i can understand. yeah me too. i love movies and traveling.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Are you American?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I love traveling to new places.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes traveling is what we are made to do as humans!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yes, i am an american.what about you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Same

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: well, just think of some happy memories and try to spend time with people who value you the most.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, thank you for the great advice!
 Like Thanksgiving, I hope you have wonderful memories with your friends and family next week.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: oh, thats nice of you.thank you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You are very welcomed!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you too!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well what are you having for lunch?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i had my breakfast just now. i am planning to cook some beef for lunch.what about you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Spicy pork tamales.. I like that not thinking about my break up makes me happy. Like talking to you about whatever else.

situation: I think my girlfriend may be cheating on me but I'm too scared to do anything about it as I don't want her to leave me. I feel ashamed of myself but I can't help it

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Good Afternoon
 How are you doing troday?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am Ok thanks but have an unusual issue. I think my girlfriend may be cheating on me but I'm too scared to do anything about it as I don't want her to leave me. I feel ashamed of myself but I can't help it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Being in a relationship with someone when you suspect unfaithful behavior is a rough thing to go through.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes it is but I should be leaving her and moving on. Problem is she is a 9 and I'm a 5 and I love her. Pretty sure she is cheating though so hopefully if I ignore it she may just leave things as they are.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I've dealt with the same problem with my partner more than once. I love him very much and I found him to be a 9 to my 5 as well. However, the question of whether he was cheating or not was destroying my trust in him and everyone else not to mention what self-respect I had... I had to break down and talk to him about it

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What happened out of interest when you had the conversation?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Ignoring this will NOT help things. There is NO relief in pretending a question or concern does not exist. Like I said the question has arisen more than once. The first time he owned to talking to other women, but nothing more.

Since that time, he denies everything.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know. I'm a typical man and I like the feeling when she is on my arm. I feel like the cat that has got the cream. It's superficial and sad I know. I knew this would never last. She is just too attractive for me. Everyone told me Iwas 'punching above my weight' and now they will all be laughing if it ends.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Honestly, its not anyone else's business. IF you're suspicions are in fact correct, then you've got to see that a loving and faithful 5 is better than a heart-breaking 10. However should you find that maybe the devils of insecurities are tricking you into thinking that she's cheating, then remember that what the two of you share o=is FOR THE TWO of you. Its a relationship, not a community project! Even mu advice, albeit in the air to help you best I can. is not more important that what matters most to YOU and HER! Curiously, why do you think she's cheating?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm almost certain of it, she is super protective of her phone and never used to be. She is always texting and vague when I ask who she is messaging. She goes away with work and when she does she rarely messages me. Recently she said when was going way for a work trip for three nights but when I called her office her colleague said they got back after two.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hmm... So have you two live together?  How long has this kind of behavior persisted? When you ask her about this behavior, how do you present the question and how does she react? Finally, how often do you talk with her about the situation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's just it. I kind of know she is cheating but I haven't approached it with her because I'm scared see will just leave and I'll be a laughing stock. I suppose I'm a joke either way!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Don't get me wrong, I completely understand why you have suspicions. Really I think anyone would in the same shoes... However, as humans, we tend to either assume the best or the worst of others. There's always a margin of error present as a variable in such situations.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: very true. I suppose I have just assumed at this stage with no real evidence. do you think I should just talk to her?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Forgive me if this comes off as disrespectful... I only want to help you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: not at all. I appreciate your candour.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: But if you feel as though you're a joke regardless, then why stay in a relationship where you're getting hurt? I know that you don't want to lose the beauty but would you not be happier with loyalty?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
All this is tied up with who and I know I'm valuing the wrong things. You are right actually. I think some of this comes from my low 
self-esteem. having a 9 helps with that. but maybe I need to deal with it directly.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I wholly believe you should gently but steadfastly confront her. I absolutely do not mean to suggest a dispute or argument. That will not help either. Simply ask her about the things that you KNOW aren't acceptable; lying about how long her trip was is one of them. Also, its okay to talk about how that stuff makes you feel, but try not to project your feelings as her fault. I know that it seems like they are... But she didn't MAKE you feel any specific way, even if her ACTIONS influenced how you were already feeling.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That seems like well considered and thoughtful advice thanks for that. What is clear is that we need to have a conversation. Thanks for helping me reach this conclusion.

situation: Raising grandson and he is being very difficult and not listening.  I also have a lot of medical issues that limit my abilities.  And my grandson's father is no help, he just drops him off and leaves, never asks if need anything or how I am doing.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi to be honest I am very depressed and angry today

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that. What is causing you these feelings?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Since helping to raise my grandson and his dad is not helping, or cares how I am feeling.  My grandson has been really difficult lately and I find myself losing my temper quickly
.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that. That sounds like a difficult situation. Have you tried any personal exercises to help you cope with the situation until you find a resolution?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do go to a quiet place and try some deep breathing, but my grandson always comes walking in

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
That's certainly a difficult situation. Is there no resolution possible with the father of your grandson? I want to make sure that th
e cause of the depression has properly been addressed by you or someone else.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I try talking to him, but due to my grandson having some disabilities his dad does not really want to be bothered.  I just want my grandson to succeed and be the best he can be

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it's great that you have such care and concern for your grandson. That shows how incredible you are and on behalf of your grandson, I think you are doing a tremendous job. I think you should continue to support him however you can, but it's also important to take care of yourself. Do you have time available for additional self care, or are there any new exercises you can try to try to take care of yourself?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thank you for saying that it means alot having someone tell me I am doing a good job. 
I recently purchased a hot tub and have been thinking that maybe when he goes to bed I could go out and relax, but have not done that yet

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like a great start. Self care is vitally important in this difficulty time with COVID and the pandemic. I recommend any little bit of time that you can contribute to yourself, this includes reading, relaxing, meditating, exercise, or just resting. Anything that can help you repair so you are better prepared to help your grandson.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I will try my best to get more time for myself and try to relax more often that I currently am.  
 I am also trying to accept that my medical issues are getting worse and I can not do all that I use to, and that truly frustrates me as well.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you think the anger you are feeling is directly a result of the situation? Do you feel the anger is manifested from somewhere else? I think it is important to get to the underlying source of the anger and take care of that first and foremost. That's concerning, but very understandable. Are you able to financially seek additional help? Sometimes it's just what you need, as hard or embarrassing as that may seem. No one needs to know what you seek, but it is important not to disregard additional help, be it mental health, physical therapy, or just general prevention and detection from a PCP.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I feel that I am having trouble dealing with the fact that I am not able to do all the things I want to do.  It is not an easy thing to lose your abilities when you feel so young but your body says otherwise.
 I was in physical therapy but could not afford it any longer.  So instead I purchased my own hot tub to be able to soak in and do some water exercises that may help me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I think that's a normal way to feel and a frustrating part of growing older. I think focusing on positives and things you enjoy will help keep your mind away from thinking about "what used to be" or "what once was" - finding passions and joys in new things could help revitalize you. 
Can I help you find new hobbies or passions? I think exercise bands are a great way to keep your limbs loose and nimble. They are affordable and available on Amazon. I have some and I think they're great. YouTube can help with exercises.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is a great idea, I may even have some here that I could use.  I am lucky that I do have two dogs that keep me going as well and am trying to get out and take them on walks more, but not easy when is so cold out lately.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Totally understandable. I'm a little younger and even I am sick of sub 50F weather, so that's not age holding you back! I think it's important to maintain positivity and find any outlet that keeps that going for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you I will definalty do that and will look for some additional exercises to do.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome. Is there anything else I can help you with today, even if it's something as simple as providing suggestions for new things to try?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would love some additional suggestions!!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Meditation has worked wonder for my best friend. The app Headspace is fantastic. I'd also recommend podcasts, using either and app or your computer, and find a series on a topic that you enjoy, weather it's mindfulness, or self-care. There are plenty to choose from and you can go at your own pace.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Those sound like great ideas, thank you again!!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome. I hope you find peace within and happiness again. Your grandson will improve with time, it's inevitable! But stay positive, stay focused, and be sure to not take anything out on him as he relies on you and your input. Have a wonderful day!

situation: A coworker feels that I am discriminating against her because I give corrective feedback on procedures she is doing wrong.  There have been numerous occasions of her wrongdoing.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I could be better.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sorry to hear. What's going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i'm having difficulty dealing with a coworker that doesn't take constructive feedback on standard work procedures well and is now starting her own investigation.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sorry to hear that. An investigation on who? you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Me and my manager. She feels that we are targeting her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's unfortunately. Have you talked to someone in upper management about this?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have talked to him about past issues with her but not this most recent. I'm trying to give myself some time to talk to him because I'm still very angry and don't want to talk with so much emotion

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is understandable. People can be hard to deal with at times. What did the co-worker do that upset you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: We have a set process of how we are to screen employees as they enter the workplace.  She doesn't follow the procedures or makes up her own way to do them because she is lazy to either stand up or greet them.  She is also rude at times to customers and has an attitude that is not friendly.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh, that's unfortunate. Have you brought this issue to upper management in the past?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have and so my manager sent a general memo reminding employees about our standard operating procedures.  And from that memo, she thinks it's only about her and that we are discriminating against her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sorry to hear. Has upper management done anything about this issue?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I haven't taken it past my immediate manager but I may have to at this point if she is looking to file a grievance or submit a statement that we are discriminating her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, in my opinion, I think you did the right thing. Has anyone else mentioned her rudeness in the past?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you.  Yes others that have worked with her share say the same thing about her rudeness and laziness on the job.  I am probably going to submit some kind of report or statement to upper management.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I was wondering, how long has she been an employee at your work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm just unsure because I have not documented the  incidents that I have had with her.  She's been employee for approximately 5 months.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No, I think you did the right thing. Wow, 5 month and she's still there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you.  With everything going on in the world right now, it's frustrating to have to deal with this person also...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, I hope everything works out for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for your help and for listening.

situation: My mom is stuck in Mexico and I hate she keeps asking me for money.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Hello.
 I am so upset. It's ridiculous how things keep happening to people around me.
  I suppose my supporter is not here.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hello how may I assist you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I am just so upset
 It's like things never get better

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that. I know how you feel.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah...
 My mom is in Mexico right now for some ex who got deported. Like come on. It's bad enough she sent
 money to him.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What have you tried in the past to turn things around?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hm. I tried moving on. And it just appears she gets worst

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you are missing your mom?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Not really...
 She just keeps asking me for mone
 I gave her like 10k for the house she bought and she hasn't done anything back.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh I see my mom has also done this in the past. Sometimes I wonder who raised who?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah. I get that
 She rented it to some guy and left before even getting some decent money
 so now she is stranded in Mexico with some guy who can't even afford to get her back

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is the worst. I ended up having to cut my mom out of my life.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I wonder if I should do the same...
 My grand mother basically gave me a whole speech about how ashamed she is.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sometimes it is for the best to set clear boundries.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
But who knows. Apparently my mom said she'll put the house in my name for all these things I did for her. 
 Kind of annoying to be frank.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is Grand mother ashamed of you or her daughter? I feel it should be her ashamed of her daughter.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
My grand mother is ashamed of her daughter
 I am kind of left with a weird circumstance.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: At least that is a good thing to come out of a bad situation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Only reason she has a house is because I helped. And if I leave it. Everyone in my family said I would be irresponsible
 to let my mom lose it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I do not think that would have a reflection on you. It should only reflect on your mom.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah, they said they already know my mother is irresponsible.
 Instead they suggested I should provide extra financial security
 to myself or at least some future children. Grandma already is giving a home and 2 apartments but... I guess I am the only kid. Christ.
 My mom lost my om over 100k to be honest. It was crazy growing up to that

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It may be nice to have a bonus house for the future. But even that comes with headaches. In the end you have to do what is best for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah. I guess boundaries are good.
 I left her without much help to be honest but I guess just hearing it frustrates
 the crap out of me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Or sell it now and pocket the money to recoup what was lost.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I would but it isn't even my property
 And mom needs a place to stay... sometimes I wonder if she just wants to be homeless

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So she is just talking circles and not accomplishing what she says?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
More like she keeps talking about doing something crazy like have a police chase
 and how she doesn't care for anything

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Some people do choose that life to be free of responsibility. Have you had a talk with her about seeing a mental health professional, She may be having some issues that would benefit from care.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I tried to baker act her after she tried to call the cops on me randomly
 It ended up them asking dumb questions like if she knows what day it is
 letting her free
 somehow ending up with her getting herself hospitalized 2 weeks later 
for low hemoglobin.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Often police officers are not fully trained in helping mentally ill people, they only access if the person is a danger to themselves or others.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yeah it sucks
 Tried to get her on disability but they just denied her
 She was brought up with bipolar depression before I was born. She said she just ran out the hospital..
 I don't know how she is just getting the consequences
 of that now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: A good first step is taking her to your local mental health provider. Or ask at a major emergency room where this is located as sometimes the places are hard to find.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
That was about 30 years ago
 Crazy they didn't stop her
 Well I do feel better. Thinking about boundaries. It has been less stressful being so involved.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Without treatment she may just be getting worse. That is good to hear..

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I've heard she has a lawyer trying to straighten up the whole disability and tr
eatment thing
 My girlfriend just won't let me stay out of it
 Saying my mom has no one and I would regret it if she just does something crazy

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That does make it difficult when a partner encourages you to engage in unhealthy behavior.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah. If it was up to me I would probably just be dead silent

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I suggest talking to your girlfriend about how it makes you feel when she insists you continue a relationship that causes so much pain.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah. That's a good point. 
 Well I think the point about boundaries really stuck with me
 I feel so much better now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It was nice talking to you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too.

situation: I don't know if I should continue with school right now and I am having a hard time making the decision.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm okay. Facing a tough decision and having a hard time coping.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that. Would you like to talk about it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, thank you. I decided to go back to school to finish my BA.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Its great that you decided to go back to school. Are you having trouble selecting a college or something like that?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well now with the pandemic I don't know if it's the right time anymore.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand your concern  given the current pandemic situation. But I have heard some colleges are offering online courses until this pandemic is over. Maybe you can explore in that direction to ease your worries.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I'm taking online courses right now, and I really don't like it. I much prefer in classroo
m lecture.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I definitely resonate with the way you feel about online learning. Although difficult it is also important to stay indoors to keep yourself safe. Is there anything that will motivate you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't know. I want to see if I could find classes conducted with Zoom or video.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe you can check with the college if they would consider offering classes over zoom as this is the most preferred mode of instruction.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will try to do that. If that's my only option right now, then I will have to settle for classes conducted over livestream. I just don't like self-learning.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sounds like a plan and am happy to see that you are sure about what you want.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I just wish it could be the way it was. I was so happy to be back in school and I'm so close to being done.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I too wish everything goes back to the way it was and it will one day. We have to adjust to a slightly new way of living and this would happen if each one of us do our part to break the chain and flatten the curve.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I agree. I still hate it though.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You can do it, as you said you are almost there. Once you do it you will be very proud of yourself. Believe me this is just a temporary setback and you are doing a great job.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thank you for the words of encouragement. I do appreciate
 that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad I could make you feel better. Is there anything else I can do today for you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No. Thanks for chatting with me.

situation: A person I was dating broke up with me in a terrible manner.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My boyfriend broke up with me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there, how are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm very upset. He yelled and yelled at me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Your relationship has ended? I am very sorry about that. And your boyfriend ended it?  That was really wrong of him if you ask me. I think you might be better off without him.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, after seeing him act this way I agree He said I was worthless.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have been in this position myself and I know how painful it is but it turned out for the best for me - I now have a great relationship!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well that's good for you, but I'm so mad right now I can't see straight!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Noone has the right to put you down like that. You really are better off without him.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know. I do deserve better. But I was with him a very long time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can you think of something to do that will take your mind off him?  How long were the two of you together?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: More than a year, but I've known him since childhood. That's what makes me so mad!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: He should not be treating you that way when you've known one another so long!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: All those years of friendship down the drain. Should I call him?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that you should call one of your girlfriends and suggest you have a movie night. It would make you feel better

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: But all of my friends are still friends with him! And they took his side!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh that is rough. That happened to me too when I broke up with my ex.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: How did you get through it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: They all took his side? Not one took yours?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not one!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I tried to find some new interests. One thing I did was take a salsa class .. that was a great way to meet new people

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm not much of a dancer, but that might be a way to meet new people.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There are lots of other things you could do. Maybe you could take an evening class in something that interests you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: He's a teacher, so taking a class might remind me of him.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think also you need to think a bit more highly of yourself and not let idiots like this treat you so badly

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: But maybe I deserved it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: he seems to have damaged your self esteem badly

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, he did.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No you did not! Please don't think like that

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm just so angry that I could break something. And maybe I will do that!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that that is a normal reaction to feeling ill treated. I hope that it will pass soon and am sure that it will.  There are a lot of therapy groups available that might be able to help you through this

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: But what if it doesn't pass? And I cannot afford therapy.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I think you really need to take it one day at a time. Things are very raw for you right now 
 There are some organisations that will offer a certain number of sessions for free

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't know if I could do that. I don't like talking about my problems with strangers.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
And yet here we are :P. Therapy can be surprisingly beneficial, I have found. 
 I do realise that it can be hard to open up to how we are feeling though. I know that I was

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Do you have any suggestions about how to open up to people I don't know?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that is useful to remember that we are all human and that most people have been hurt in this way at some point. No-one will be judging you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's what I am afraid of. Being judged by people.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you think that this might partly because your ex has eroded your self esteem?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Probably. He called me a lot of names.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: The important thing is to gradually rebuild your self esteem. Do you have any things that you excel at?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm good at bowling. And I like riding horses.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Animals are a wonderful source of comfort when we are feeling very low! Could you join a riding club to make some new friends?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Maybe I will do that. Horses are so cool.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can feel that you are starting to make progress already and are thinking of things that you can do post break up

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I sure am. Thanks for your help.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are most welcome. Is there anything else you would like to talk to me about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't think so...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, I wish you all the very best for the future. I know that things seem terrible right now but please believe me they will get better

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks! Have a good day.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And you have a lovely day too. I know that you are going to be just fine in a few weeks from now. Stay strong!

situation: I am so mad that my boyfriend broke up with me because I cheated on him once. He has cheated on me many times and I never broke up with him.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am so mad that I can't see straight.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello! What is on your mind today?  Oh really? Why is that?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, my boyfriend has cheated so many times that I cant count them. I then meet someone nice and cheat on him once and now he breaks up with me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: being mad that your boyfriend cheated is normal. being mad when dumped is normal.  don't you think you DESERVE better than he was treating you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you! I can see that I am talking to someone who understands. I am wondering if I should beg him back?? I really do love him.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I've been there too Expecially with covid, it can be hard realiing you may be alone and it may be frusterating dating someone new if you really love someone, shouldnt they love you back? did his actions show love?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, and we have been together since high school so it is really hard to think of ever marrying anyone else. He asked me to marry him but we never made it official or got rings.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: if you did get back together and get married, how do you know he wouldnt cheat again? what if you had kids? they would have to see you go through heartbreak i think its normal to want to marry your high school sweetheart

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: He told me he never wants kids with me but acts like he would have them someday, somehow? I think you're right.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i married mine. 3 years later he left me to move into his girlfriends house leaving me with our two month old daughter trust me, its not worth it in the long run maybe you can find someone deserving on someone like you!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Noway! That is my worst nightmare, I am so sorry. That is a real eye opener for me/ That is exactly what I don't think I could handle. I just am in the worst cycle ever and don't have any friends to turn to or anybody who wants to listen to the drama. I think I have wasted a lot of my life and I can't ever get the time back that I have invested in him.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i think a lot of couples are feeling stuck with covid. once things open up and everyone is vaccinated and get togethers happen again, i bet there will be a LOT more breakups and a lot more options out there! all we can do is learn from our mistakes and not let the past repeat itself

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, I can see your point. It is nice being able to vent to you and feel like I am being heard. I never got that in my relationship with him. I need to learn from the past this time and stop repeating it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: with your newly aquired free time, you can focus on you! start a new hobby, dye your hair,do something you always wanted to do!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I haven't even had the time to get myself cute and fixed up in forever! I love that idea! I need to get my haircut.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: know what the best part is?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, I have idea?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: get yourself fixed up and cute, and find a hot new guy. your ex will see on social media and know he messed up and see you happy. its quite theraputic :D

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: HAHA!! YES!! I was thinking the exact same thing! The best revenge is moving on and looking good and with somebody hot!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: EXACTLY! You deserve so much better! Make yourself feel like it and everyone see it! :D :D

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Sounds great! I am revived and am going to schedule a spa day and reinvent myself! Out with the old! Thank you for your encouragement today. I am going to move on from this mess and make my life better. Thanks again!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good luck!

situation: The partiality in my work place and the favorability to people who hardly work but always say "yes" to my manager's say.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Hi
 Im feeling down and need some advice as to how do I handle this situation

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hello how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I'm doing ok
 How about you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm doing well. Ok I can help. Whats going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's to do with some of my peers who hardly work but are favorable to my Manager as they always say yes to his word.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you are unsure of how to deal with favoritism in the workplace. I have dealt with similar situations.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, it bothers me as I cannot tolerate injustice

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: When did this start?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: for about a year

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can you tell me some examples about the injustice?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Some colleagues are given an option to log out early, whereas some of us have to slog .

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is the logging out early a reward for their work? I had a job that offered similar rewards based on performance.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, it's a privilege given to them if they agree to anything the Manger says like going out with him for a drink, etc..

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is very inappropriate. This must be very hard for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is, I try to let it go, but fail at times.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If your manager has a supervisor that you feel would not approve of this behavior, I would speak to them. If you don't feel that would be beneficial to you, you could reach out to your company HR department.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I have that as my last resort. What do you think? Should I go ahead as I'm afraid my step may backfire me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would not be able to let this go either. I'm so sorry you're in such an unfair and unethical work environment. Do you know the supervisor well or interact with them? It could backfire if they both interact with this behavior. I believe you will make the right decision. If it does backfire, this is a toxic work environment and not a good fit for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes
 Thanks for your inputs
 Feels better when you share with someone who has similar experience and can understand your situation

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are very welcome. It does feel good to communicate with people who can understand how you feel. I wish you the very best of luck.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you so much. Take care. Be safe.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome. You be safe as well.

situation: My uncle passed away a couple of days ago and I have really been going through it because I have lost 5 other family members this year

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you doing?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Hi, I'm doing alright I suppose 
 how about yourself?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am doing well. I've been handling the pandemic surprisingly well given I deal with germaphobia myself. I can't wait for this all to be over. Is there something I can help you with today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah, the anxiety of being trapped in my apartment has kind of gotten to me but I am doing the best I can to keep myself occupied 
 Just really wanted somebody to talk to I suppose

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah, socially distancing is quite the isolating experience so I can relate to how you are feeling. What have you been doing to keep yourself occupied?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Playing video games mostly, I try to exercise everyday and cook meals at home as oppose to eating out.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It's good that you are doing your best to take care of yourself. Have you been able to connect with friends or loved ones?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Not really, but my mother and sister live close by

 I visit them almost every other day and I have a couple of friends who swing by now and again

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It seems like you are doing what you can to stay connected with people. Did something happen that lead you to want to reach out for support?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, I dealt with my heavy drinking and drug use problems at the beginning of the pandemic

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you were going through a really difficult time. What was going on for you at the start of the pandemic?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Just really lonely, lost my job financial woes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can imagine how that would put you in a dark place in your life. Just know that you are not alone in this and that there are resources and opportunities out there.

situation: I live with my ex and he's having a break down and my current boyfriend lives far away and is struggling with addiction and money problems. I feel constantly worn down.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hello. how have you been feeling lately?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Tired
 I want to sleep all my problems away

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: how long has this been going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Well the boyfriend for awhile the other thing for a day or two
 I got a hold of a therapist but they won't talk to me for two days

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: that's very good that you are going to talk to a therapist.  you showed great strength by reaching out.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am hoping it helps because I feel helpless, especially today and on edge

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: have you been sleeping mostly during the day, or at night?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I can't sleep through the day because I have kids but when my ex gets home I just go hide and try to sleep so it's typically late afternoon

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: it sounds like you are doing the very best you can under difficult circumstances.  you should feel very proud about that.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you i feel very tired tho. i feel like i want every day to go by faster. the relationship i am in is a long distance relationship and i am supposed to go home to see him in ten days and i just want them to be over.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: it sounds to me that your sleep issue is really weighing on your mind.  you may be setting yourself up for sleep issues by thinking about this so much. is that possible?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes it may be. i'm not sure. i sometimes wake up panicking.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you're under a lot of pressure.  it's only natural that you would feel overwhelmed.  dont be hard on yourself.  you're doing the best you can.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you. that is very supportive i just dont know how to make the next ten days go by faster

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: perhaps you can spend some time doing pleasant things.  it is true that time seems to go fast when we're having fun.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that's true. i thought about maybe playing video games

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: fantastic! that's a great way to spend your time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: maybe overwatch i haven't been playing them much

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i'm wondering: are you a reader? do you enjoy reading? i'm asking because i find when i read a good book, time seems to move very quickly.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i am. but i have a hard time focusing as of late.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: perhaps you can make a goal of reading a few pages and then see if you can build on that.  then,if you want, you can make a goal of reading a chapter.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that's a great idea. that may help a lot with focus.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: personally i love reading; especially non fiction. what type of books do you enjoy?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: dystopian fantasy style books. and also historical nonfiction.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i enjoy historical non fiction very  much.  do you have any favorite authors?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
lauren destefano
 her series the chemical garden trilogy is my favorite

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: has she written anything you haven't read yet that you think you would enjoy?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that's also another good idea. i haven't read all of her bookis

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: do you live near a library?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i do but they're closed because of covid

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: there may be some libraries in your area that are open, or have reduced hours.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i can try the bookstores are open

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: wonderful! do you live near a book store?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: sorta but not that close but i can take the bus out there

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you can call the store and see if they have anything written by your favorite author.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that's a great idea

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: getting back to the sleep issue, have you considered thinking some pleasant thoughts before going to bed?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i try but i get too stresed

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i can understand that.  the stress is interfering with your thoughts but you are still doing a good job. you are doing the best you can do; that's the best anyone can do.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you. that's true.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i'd like to offer a suggestion.  please try, as best as you can, NOT to think about the sleep issue.  millions of people have sleep issues. you are not alone.  try thinking about other things, and if your mind comes back to the sleep problem, try thinking about something else.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you i will take that advcie

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: and make sure you are not hard on yourself.  you're getting by. you're hanging in there.  you are doing well under the circumstances.

situation: I am severely stressed about my college course and my grades during the pandemic.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am so stressed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, what's going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I have a college course in the evening, but I cannot concentrate while distance learning.
 I am expected to watch the instructor do excel on a tiny phone screen and follow along.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oooff that sounds brutal... especially the excel part! do you have a bigger screen you can use?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It is stressful. I am able to pull up zoom on my laptop, but it is almost more cumbersome because I must switch back and forth. I have to also manipulate an excel as well as watch the instructor.
 My colleagues in the class are annoying, because they do not know how to use mute correctly.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you have access to a second screen or have the ability to stream the class on your tv through chromecast or something?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't have a second screen, but I do have a large TV. I may be able to hook it up via HDMI.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: The mute thing is horrible. I have no ideas for that. hahah. I'm looking for help with that too! Yeah try the HDMI hook up you can sometimes find cheap attachements on amazon if you don't have the right connections.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Sometimes I get really anxious. My ears will be pounding and I feel like I can't hear anything.
 It is worse when using in ears, because it's like all I can hear is my heartbeat.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yeah if you stream it on your tv, you won't have to wear headphones... that way you can control the volume better too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That sounds like a great idea. Have you had any weird technological solutions to help you through distance learning?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm not really doing any distance learning right now. Just working  from home and coincidentally most of my work uses excel. I have constant virtual meetings where I have to show people things via excel. It's pretty tough but I found if I use multiple monitors it makes things easier.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:

situation: my partner broke up with me. i have no idea how am going to handle it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi! How are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i don't want to lie. am not good for sure. my boyfriend broke up with me. how could he do this to me?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear this. When did your boyfriend break up with you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: last week. we were like  a lovey - dovey couple. it hurts

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Breakups always hurt. They can be very painful, how long were you together?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: almost for 2 years. this is sad right?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It's very sad and your emotions will take a toll, but you have to set some goals so you don't fall into a funk. Do you have a good support system? Did he give a reason for the breakup?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: no. he didn't told me anything. avoiding me all the time. i don't have a support system either.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you telling me he just said "I am breaking up with you" then never said why and left?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: he thinks everything is casual . we were happy. he is behaving like this all of a sudden. what should i think then ?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think he doesn't know what he wants and thinks the grass is greener on the other side of the fence. It is disrespectful to you. And hurtful. You say you have no support, don't you have friends? Family?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i don't have a family. i am living alone in a city where am working. i have not met anyone because of the pandemic.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think you might want to join an online support group then. Do you have any goals or aspirations that you are working on apart from your relationship? Because it will be painful to move forward, but if you have some goals, that can help you from falling into a slump.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i think i should set some goals and engage in daily activities like meditation and yoga. i am thinking about contacting a medical assistance too. i can't overcome it by myself. i am really grateful for your support and kind words. i feel somewhat positive. i was in need of someone like this.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are welcome. I am happily married now, but I had my fair share of breakups. They hurt my self esteem. I had to work hard to not let that happen. And do my best to grieve, but not get stuck there.  If a man did not want to be with me, I took it hard as rejection, but in the long run, if he does not want to be with me, I don't want to be with him, but that takes time, and you need friends to encourage you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: you really are a strong person.  yes. if i follow him, i will be the one to get hurt. its time for me to accept the reality.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you. I'm older, been through a LOT of hard knocks. You will be stronger too, right now you are hurting, in time you will see more clearly.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hoping the same. i should be strong and take this as an experience to learn.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: He hasn't told you why, so the problem lies in him, not you. So do your best not to be hard on yourself. It is a shock to you now, but he never even communicated this with you over time, and he should have. that's what you want in a partner.  If a relationship has problems, you want someone to talk about he problems and work on them and grow as two people. He chose to walk away, that's immature and not what you want in a relationship. That is actually abusive.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes. its better to be alone than with a partner who didn't understand us. really you are awesome mam ! there is a lot to learn from you ! i feel like you can clearly see through my mind.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you! You are so very kind! And you are very sweet too, willing to hear the truth! That is an admirable quality!  You deserve a partner willing to work on problems, not a quitter.  Do take care of yourself! Find some friends, work hard at that, call old friends too. Just to say hi, call an old aunt or uncle and check in. Find community. That will help you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i should take a break from this relationship issues and try to enjoy myself and heal from this. you are kind too mam !. your partner is really lucky to have you. sure mam. i will really try hard.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes! Don't hop into another relationship for a long time, months, even a year. Find out who you are. Be the woman you want to be. I prayed for my husband for a long time. God is good! :D I had a lot of bad ones before him!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: we all learn from our experiences. yes. i want to be emotionally independent. i should take a break and travel after this pandemic. hope you will be happy as well.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you so much!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i should be one thanking you mam!. take care.good night and have a deep sleep.
