situation: I don't have enough money to buy my kids christmas gifts.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How are things going?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not good. I'm depressed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything in particular that has been bothering you recently?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I don't have enough money to buy my 5 kids X-mas gifts.
 Hello?
 Are you still here?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm really sorry to hear that, but I'm sure your love for your kids can still shine through

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do love my kids, but they are expecting some toys and stuff from Santa.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Would it be possible to look at some more home made stuff for this year that will still be fun and meaningful?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I cannot craft. If I made them something it would be so bad.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know it can be really hard to feel like you can't make anything worthwhile, but I'm sure theres something you could come up with that could still put a smile on your kids faces

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Do you have any craft ideas?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe you could look at involving your kids in the process. The feeling of doing something together as a family can make it way more fun. It could be a shared experience your family can look back on fondly later on.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's a good idea. I would like to have it done before christmas. How about some wooden toys or a cardboard box fort?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have seen some people make small ornaments filled with candies and treats and other small goodies. So even if all of the gifts feel small, they can feel like they are getting a lot of them. And you could even get them to have fun decorating and painting the ornaments! Yeah, a box fort is really fun!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I never that about filling an ornament with small stuff. That's a great idea.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: When I was a kid, I loved making box forts, and its a great way to reuse stuff you might already have around the house. The fun pretend games you can play were a blast. I'm sure your kids might love it too!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I could paint and decorate it. My daughters would like that. I can make it look like a castle.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It can feel tough when it feels like you can't provide all the big gifts that you feel your kids deserve But you clearly care and love your kids, and want to do something for them

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do. this has been very helpful. It's not all hopeless.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Last Christmas when I gave gifts to my family, one of them later told me that the gift they loved the most was the box of Christmas cookies that they were able to enjoy over the course of the week afterwards, and not the keyboard I had gotten them. Sometimes it doesn't take something expensive to be meaningful and make someone happy

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That sounds wonderful. I'm getting to work on my projects. Thank you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm really glad to hear that. It still might feel tough, but truly things are not hopeless

dialog.content: I see. Well it's nice that we matched up! I actually work at a college and now a couple good strategies to get off academic probation, and or avoid it! What is it you are studying?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You're a great resource then! I am majoring in Psychology with a minor in Biology.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh that is awesome! So when do you find out whether or not you are on probation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Likely in the next week.
 I registered for my classes for next semester but I think I should drop my minor. \

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Okay gotcha. Well a couple tips I can offer you: If you do end up on probation, most courses offer a probation workshop you can go through. And if you graduate in June, it should finish in time for you! And I was also going to recommend taking fewer units. As much as it sucks, you can't overwhelm yourself with work. You need to be able to have a healthy and happy life that is separate from school in order to preform your best. Dropping your minor may be what is best for not only your grades, but your mental health
 I also recommend making an educational plan. Although it can seem scary and long to see your goals directly laid out in front of you, it really helps with motivation

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
That's great advice and likely would be my best option.
 I will definitely do that. I feel better hearing that it's okay to drop my minor. I felt like a failure when I considered it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: From talking with you so far, I can tell that you are stressed about this. But I want to reassure you that your mental health should always come first. It is the most important thing you can do.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes I am very stressed. I hate taking online courses and this pandemic is wearing me out.
 Plus I have a 2 year old son.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are definitely not a failure in the slightest! In fact, I would argue that you are the exact opposite of that. Being able to take such difficult courses in a pandemic, and raise a child shows how strong you are!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for saying that. It is tough, but I just want to finally finish my Bachelor's and do something positive.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
And you will succeed in that goal. You have the motivation and the care for it, you just need to focus on managing everything at once!
 Have you thought about meeting with an academic advisor and laying out a plan?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes. I had plans to. I am going to go an make an appointment immediately.
 I appreciate your time, encouragement, and support.

situation: I decided to go back to school to complete my Master's degree, but I feel really overwhelmed between being a parent, having a career, and going to school at the same time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello, I have been feeling very overwhelmed with school.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello. I totally understand being stressed out by school. I am currently in a Ph.D. program and it can be overwhelming. Do you have any hobbies to help with your stress?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do like to read, but I haven't had enough time. I have been going on walks more frequently.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you do a lot of activities that are solitary. Have you considered maybe partaking in a similar activity, like a book club, where there is more interaction with people?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, I did not consider that actually. I do enjoy my alone time since I am so busy and I have a toddler, but maybe doing something more sociable would be good.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Being busy, especially with having a small child can make you feel lonely, enhancing the stress you feel from school. Participating in social events geared toward your interest might be able to connect you to people with similar feelings!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think so too. I never thought of it like that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sure that you are trying your best to be both a good student and a great parent. It is understandable that you may not have considered those options.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes I constantly think of my son and family, and I didn't think involving myself in sociable activities
 would be beneficial. I figured I really needed some alone time. I didn't realize I could be experiencing loneliness.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It is so admirable that you are acting so selfless. Are you aware of any activities that you can participate in that can include your family? It could be the best of both worlds.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, we like to go swimming at the YMCA together.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is great! Physical activity has been shown to reduce stress levels. My sister-in-law really benefits from having her sons in sports. She is able to connect with the other team moms and considers them some of her best friends. Depending on your YMCA your son may be able to participate in some of their programs?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, and swimming is great exercise.
 And if we do that more we can spend more time together.
 I have been looking into putting him in soccer.
 He can start at 3 years old.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Soccer would be a perfect way for both you and your son to interact with other people of similar interests.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think so too. You gave me a lot to think about today that I didn't consider before.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad to help, Hopefully, considering these options will not only reduce your stress from being busy with being a parent and with school, but it will also bring you closer to your child.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, and both of those are major goals of mine. But the stress sometimes feels inevitable.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sometimes stress is inevitable. You just have to know how to channel it. Some use physical activity, others use journaling. These seem to be outlets you can consider?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: The physical activity for sure. I do so much writing with my Master's program. Sometimes I feel like my hand will fall off.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Physical activity seems to be the option you are most interested in. Have you considered setting daily exercise goals?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, I am a pretty active and fit person.
 Yes, I do try to go to the gym 3 times per week at least.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Having goals for a given day can help motivate you to do the exercises and keep your head cleared from stress.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I agree. I will definitely start to engage in more activities with peers and more swimming with my family. Thank you for your advice!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Your welcome.

situation: My friend totally screwed me over. We were doing a business deal and he lied about how much money we got from the deal. He screwed me!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi, how are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm doing well. How are things going with you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm really pissed off. My friend totally screwed me over. We were doing a business deal and he lied about how much money we got from the deal. He screwed me!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh no, that must be so frustrating for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: We made a piece of software together. It was sweet. We were marketing it to different firms. We thought we could make a bundle.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you were partners in the development of the software and both put in a lot of work? Did you have any indication that he might try to defraud you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
The stupid SOB went behind  my back and cut a deal. I didn't get as much as I should have. I totally didn't suspect anything because we had been friends for so long. 
 I guess the money was too sweet. Never again...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So it feels like he took advantage of your friendship. That must be hard, but don't beat yourself up about it. Let's come up with some safeguards for the future to avoid more incidents.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Good point. I feel like a fool, but it could happen to anyone. 
 Safeguards. Ok.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What do you think you might have done differently? You can only control your behavior, not the other guy's. Could you have put a contract in place? Or have a shared Google drive where you both put all the information relevant to the development and marketing? You doing okay? It was nice talking with you, and I do hope I helped you feel more in control of your next business venture. It's okay to feel that anger at your (former) friend, too. Use that feeling to work through things and I'm sure you'll be feeling more optimistic soon.

situation: Ex girlfriend issues.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi! How is life treating you right now?  (apologies for the delay, I was afk)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: ITs been better. I am dealing with issues in life.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What sort of issues are you having? I am happy to listen

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: she was giving her kids who were 12 and 15 drugs, I turned her in to CPS for it. The relationship ended in a fury and  a mess.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Who is she? A partner, a sister or similar? I am glad you turned her in, whoever she was.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
girlfriend. 
 well ex girlfriend.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a really lousy thing for her to have done. I am glad that you did the right thing!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Shes pretty furious from what I know. Shes trying to stalk me and find where I moved to. I left the state i lived in over all this. shes been known to be a bit violent and crazy in her reactions.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you considered getting a restraining order against her? That should be quite easy to do

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: it would then give her my address to where I live, which is what I fear most. as she then knows right where I am. the whole reason i left was to have her not know my location.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hope that she would not be informed as to where you live though yes, I understand the dilemma. Have you spoken with the police?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have. I looked into it. and if i file a restraining order, it will give her my address. To supress my address it would take charges filed and things of the sort. Shes crafty.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow that sounds like it has put you in a bind. I do understand cos once I had to flee from a boyfriend and was scared he would come after me.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: She will definately come after me. I have evidence that makes her guilty that has been turned into the attorneys and cps. Shes losing custody of her daughter who is 12. The 15yr old lives with the father. so she wont lose custody, but im sure her visits will be messed up.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you still in contact with her kids? Do you have a good relationship with them?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am not. I am not planning on it either. Staying away is the best plan.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It might help them a lot to have someone stable in their life though?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
they have fathers. I am not involved like that. we had a short relationship and I am sure her girls will be bitter with me even tho i did the correct thing by turning her in. 
 but the guilt from turning her in is terrible.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You did the right thing. Would you have wanted the girls to end up as addicts, dead or in prison?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: She was not a good person. So it makes it easy to not regret it. but i still feel guilty. guilt caused issues last month when this all started and came to a head. it was a mess. I was a mess. things happened.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you think that perhaps the guilt is not entirely related to your circumstances currently?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I dont believe so. The guilt to me sets with causing a disruption between mother and daughters. It created a large burden for me to bear.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know it is difficult but I hope that you can manage to move on from this and find happiness.  Thank you for talking and I wish you all the best.

situation: Well we are at different places in our lives. My partner wants to leave to go to another state to be with her cousin. I want to stay around the family we have here already.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm doing ok

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's good to hear. Is there anything that you would like to talk about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, me and my partner are considering breaking up as she wants to move to another state to be with her cousin and have a fresh start. I want to stay and be around my family. So that is the dilemma.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So it sounds to me like you're experiencing stress about choosing between staying in your current relationship, or staying close to your family. Do I have that right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes and its very hard for me to make a decision on which to choose.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand how that feels. Anybody would have a hard time deciding between those options. May I ask you a question about your relationship?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, sure

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Compared to other relationships that you've had in the past, how strongly do you feel about this one?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would say compared to every relationship I have had, this is the best relationship I have been in. Plus we have children involved.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That helps me understand that this is quite a problem, however talking to you now I feel like you can trust yourself to eventually reach the best decision.  Have you talked this out with your family and your partner together?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think that after speaking with you, I realize the choice I must make though difficult. And yes we have talked with everyone close with us or connected.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How did that go? Did everybody feel like they had their voice heard in a cooperative manner?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel like everyone respected both of our wishes as they were different but everyone also offered advice and shared their experiences. Everyone was cooperative.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you had a cooperative conversation, but not a very productive one, is that right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes we listened to each other but it was still a more agree to disagree conversation. No over talking or arguments.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand how those conversations go, believe me we all have them. They don't seem to solve much a first, but we better off doing it than not.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I have come to understand that we must have those hard conversations rather than avoiding it. Thanks for the talk!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome, I hope you have a better understanding of the problem now.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do now that I have talked it through.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's good to hear, may I ask how the weather is today?

situation: recently i lost my job

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I lost my job recently that's why I'm very sad.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello So you lost your job that is why you are sad right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm very sad about my situation

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay so what kind of job was it? And why do you think you lost your job?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm very sad because in this coronavirus situation its very difficult to get new job   because of this coronavirus situation

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay yes I agree with you I was also trying to find a new job since I had more time after graduating.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes i now its very difficult time for everyone  Now I'm looking for new job

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay since you are trying to find a new job you could try to learn some new techniques online. So you could put that on your resume to keep you from feeling sad.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Now I'm waiting for to when government will opening all business Thanks, Yes i will hope i can come from this situation  yes I'm taking some trainings online

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh that is good. That will make you different from other people so when government opens up it will be easier for you to get in. So I would say to keep your hope up by trying to learn new things so you do not feel sad.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I have hope and i'm thinking positive Thanks for giving me support

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah that is the best medicine for right now. Your welcome. Thank you for giving your time to talk to me.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm still struggling to get new job that's why I'm sad

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah in this economy right now not that many places are hiring right now so it is hard. Or they are hiring but a lot of people are applying so they are looking at everybody and choosing the best canidate.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes its very difficult time i know, Thanks for your support

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Your welcome.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: good to having you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks

situation: I just got my job and I heard they have to let people go because business is slow.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hello. I'm very nervous about my job

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hello. im so sorry to hear you're nervous about your job. what are you nervous about specifically?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Well, I just finally got my job about a month ago. I haven't worked for most of covid. But, I think they may start letting go staff because its getting really slow FYI, it's new york, and it has gotten really cold. People might be worried about getting sick.
 i mean regular sick, as well as covid

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you are feeling worried about potentially losing your job? That does sound very difficult. Especially after not working for months and just finally getting a job. I personally lost work for a bit of time due to covid. It can be tough, especially in New York things are not that great health-wise.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's true, but they're not as bad as they were. I feel pretty safe. They make us get tested twice a week I can't be unemployed again. I just got on a lease!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad they make you get tested and you feel safe. That is the most important thing.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It was like one day we were soooo busy that we had a waiting list the whole day, then the next it was crickets
 It didnt help when they forced us to start closing by 10
 We just created this amazing winter wonderland and i get to go in this little hut out there and stand by a heater and serve people holday drinks all by myself. its amazing
 i cant lose that

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like an amazing job!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I'm depressed just thinking about it. I only got to work in there because its only for christmas. Before I had to stand outside by the door under a heat lamp and seat people. But I didnt mind
 Should i pre-emptively start looking for new jobs?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is the job good only temporarily? Like do you aim to keep this job or is it only a holiday job?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I plan to keep it as long as possible. Its the most amazing working environment I've ever had
 Would it be unprofessional if I ask my manager if my job is in danger?
 What do you think i should do?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How would you approach asking the manager if your job is in danger if you were to do that?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Idk, really. What should I say?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, I remember once being unsure of my job. I approached my manager and with all professionalism, I asked if there was any way I can do more for the work. That helped me at the time. I never specifically asked if my job was in danger, though.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: so you mean, demonstrate my willingness to be helpful?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah, exactly.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Ok thats great advice

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you feel comfortable talking to your manager?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes. thank you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad I was able to help. I hope I helped you feel at least a little better today.

situation: I fear disappointing my parents.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hi, can I discuss an issue with you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes please. What has been bothering you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My parents, they expect me to always get straight A's.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: oh wow. Is that hard for you? Explain what's upsetting you about that.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is upsetting because it is an expectation.  I am not perfect and I always try my hardest.  I am afraid of not being perfect.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I've also struggled with the same thing. My parents put that pressure on me so I understand that.  Do you tell your parents you're trying your best? What do they say?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They tell me it is my only job.  They think it will help pay for college if I am the best.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like that expectation is very hard on you and makes you anxious That's okay to feel that way!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks, I just don't want to disappoint them. They work so hard and want the best for me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Deep down they really just want you to succeed. But that doesn't mean it isn't difficult for you. I'm sure you're doing your best. Have you talked to them about the pressure/expectation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am, I told my sister to not get straight a's or they will always expect it.  I h ave not talked to them

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would suggest talking to them about the expectation. You could say something like, "I want you guys to realize that I am trying my best but when you put this expectation on me and makes me even more stressed out"

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay, I can try that, it makes me very anxious.  Would writing a letter be okay?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a scary conversation to have. I think the letter idea is a great idea!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am better at expressing my feelings that way.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm better writing things down too.  Writing that should hopefully let them see what is going on. I think it will work.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I hope it will work, maybe I will write it and leave it in the morning for them to find after I go to school.  That way they can think about what I said and not just react.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's an even better idea. Great thinking!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks, you have made me feel a lot better.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm glad. I hope it all works out for you!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: me too, bye

situation: Job safety is causing my sadness and stress. COVID-19 is making it worse.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi, how are we doing today?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Im doing good. How are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not so good unfortunately.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh no! I'm sorry to hear that. Would you be willing to talk about it with me? Sometimes talking with someone is the help that we need.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Due to this pandemic, I fear for the safety of my job. It's cause me anxiety and stress.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can sense how stressed you are about the safety of your job. This pandemic has caused so much stress and very much anxiety on everyone, so just know you are not alone. I'm not sure on the work you do, but you putting your health at risk everyday to make a living for you and your family makes you an amazing person. Just try and remember that your health is very much more important then your job and just make sure you are trying everything possible to keep yourself safe out there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for the positive vibes, but I doubt things will get better.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Of course! I also am afraid that this pandemic is going to get worse before it gets any better and that is just my opinion. I am scared for everyone out here anymore and its not just people getting sick and ill, but people like you who could be in jeopardy of loosing their job or their source of income. That's is something that would be on anyones mind, more yours then mine due to you actually working in the public.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: There is a lock down in my area, I fear it will only get worse.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: We thankfully have not got on lockdown just yet, but I know it will be happening soon. We live in a very small town and it has got bad here that either we know someone who has it, or someone in the family has it. It seemed like it just happened over night that it just spread so fast here that everyone is in a panic mode again. What type of things do you do while you are on lockdown?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Think about the bad things that will happen if I lost my job. I have been only thinking about the bad..

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can only imagine. Is it yourself in your home? What I have tried to do is stay away from the news that has anything to do with covid that could maybe help a little. And god forbid anything happens to you and you do loose your job just know there are a lot of resources around that would help you in any struggles that you fear or end up facing. I know its hard and stressful to search and look for things like that but trust me it will take a lot of stress off your mind.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am not religious, but thanks for the uplifting words. I just don't see a bright side over the fence.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry about that I didn't realize I put that, sometimes it just comes out but I do apologize. I know it's hard to think that this will not come to and end, but just try and think there is always a light at the end of the tunnel, and I'm hoping for you and what your doing and putting in, something will come out positive in your favor, even if its just a small step forward. Hey its all gotta start out somewhere.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No worries. Any activity suggestions to keep my mind off of COVID-19?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you have children or a spouse that lives with you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, this is why, if I lose my job, my financial situation will affect the family.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have children as well, and what I have tried to do is like a at home drive in movie night, or simple board games. I don't have a spouse but I would think maybe an inside dinner night or even a lunch date. Have the kids even help out with that. You'll be amazed on how excited they would be even if it's the little things they can help with like the seating or drawing of the menus. And for maybe the inside drive in make up little movie tickets, and candy and popcorn like a little concession stand.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for the suggestions. This pandemic has really taken a toll on me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I completely understand. I know its hard not to think about it because it is EVERYWHERE, but if your spouse or children see or sense the stress its causing you they can also feel it, and can start stressing over it too. Even trying these things will cause a smile and a fun time for those moments that will make every one happy and not think about covid, because they will all be thinking about how much fun they are having with you and the family, and that will help with the stress even if its just a little bit.

situation: Well, my wife of 10 years just decided to request a divorce, which is very unexpected.  She is saying that she is moving out with the two kids.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, it has been a hard week. My wife decided to separate from me with the two klids.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Whats been going on? Oh no, that's not good

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am disgusted....just like President Donald Trump is with the $600 dollar stimulus checks.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ain't that the truth.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It just isn't my fault. My wife had no right to leave California without me. I made a mistake making California my home.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sure it isn't, how long had you been together? California is beautiful I know, but it can also be pretty quick paced.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: We were together 10 years.  I am just disgusted that it had to happen at this point. Christmas week, of all weeks.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you still working?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am still working right now.  Luckily, California has plenty of money for bureacrats lie myself. I contributed to the marriage for sure. I love my wife and my great kids

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It's important that you know that you can feel whatever way you need to about your loss. Its a tough time of year for something like that. Had things been pretty rocky?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Things like this were sudden.  I mean, I never expected her to up and walk out with the kids. The only thing I can think of was that California was getting too expensive and the homeless were taking over LA; that may have spooked her to leave the state.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow, that's horrible. It seems you should have some kind of legal authority in that aspect. Is that a big problem out there?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I really don't have any discourse.  The kids are almost 18.  They are twins. Yeah, in her eyes, she seems fed up with the state. She left me for Texas, the state, not the person. I mean, she never mentioned loving, "Text," but the kids are bing into the Dallas Cowboys

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It is important that you understand that it seems that she has been thinking of this for a while.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I overhead her say that Texas is a free state and my twin boys do love the Dallas Cowboy's team.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Does she have family there?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, she does. Both her parents live in Laredo.....big spread, too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you spoken with her since she left?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, she left two days ago.  I came home and the furniture and my widescreen TV were all gone. We lived in a small apartment in LA.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's mid-boggling..

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am D-I-S-G-U-S-T-E-D to the Nth degree.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you have any hobbies?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Any help to get her to come back would help. My hobbies are on hold.....I need to get "un-disgusted" first.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, I can't make her come back, but I can provide you with some information on how to get through these tough times.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: ok. I am all ears.....

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand, it can be good to help get some stress out in other ways. Do you excercise? What kind of hobbies do you have?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have a gym membership....if they are even open with the COVID-19. Maybe I can just jog or speed walk every day.  Perhaps pushups can help?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I like to run every morning, and it seems to help make alot of stress evaporate.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh, that may just help me relax and think about other things besides my wifey and twin kiddos. Well, your input has helped.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have found for me anyway, that running is an amazing stress reliever.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will mull your insights over.  Good bye.

situation: I am currently studying for an exam that I need to do for work.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello! How can I help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am anxious about an upcoming test.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
It's normal to feel nervous whenever we have an important assessment coming.
 I myself would always be really anxious whenever I had any finals back in college.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I have a bunch of courses that I need to do for my work, it certainly keeps me busy.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would suggest that you study as much as you can. The more prepared you are for that test, the more manageable you will find it and the less stressed you will feel.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I am pretty good about that but it just kills my free time because I am working full time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. However, you need to be able to find some time dedicated to your school activities. You are the one who needs to decide how to insert it in your schedule.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes honestly I am the main driver behind me doing all this schooling it is just going to be good for my career and I don't just want to stand still.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I agree, once you have your career set up you won't have to worry about working and studying anymore. You will be doing what you like for a good living. Think about the future.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is certainly the plan

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Just remember not to stress too much about it. It's just one test at the end of the day, you can do this!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I am not super worried about it it is more the stress from not having free time, also insurance textbooks are not extremely exciting.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know that I told you to try to fit school time into your schedule, but the same goes with leisure time for yourself. Find an activity that you enjoy and try to dedicate at least an hour a day to it, be it exercising, playing videogames, reading a book.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes that is good advice, I try to find time for myself but time seems to disappear sometimes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Happens to all of us. I myself sometimes get too invested in work and the days go by without me realizing it, but it should definetely be a priority. Try to maximize your efficiency so you can do your work while having more time for other activities.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is helpful, I am just glad I will have a few days off after today

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
That's absolutely great, make good use of them (^_^)
 Is there anything else I can help you with today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
You have been very helpful, sorry I left for a bit
 Have a wonderful day:)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No worries, you too!

situation: My boyfriend broke up with me after we lost our baby 4 months in.

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: okay, is there anything you can do to take your mind off things? or speak to anyone about it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: okay, have you ever tried meditation?

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: okay, I understand

dialog.content: am trying to make sure no one notices so cant secretly see a therapist

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Why don't you want nobody to find out, if I might ask?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: because if we do get back together my family or friends might not agree with it or support it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: okay, isn't it best to do what you think is right though?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What I think is right might hurt other people like how people view him

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: okay fair enough Why do you still want him even though, he broke up with you if you don't mind me asking?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: its hard to let go of everything after all we've had

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: it might be hard, but isn't it better to see how things go without him. It's not good to be a relationship if someone isn't 100 percent dont you agree?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: it feels like loosing an important part of my body

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: it might feel like that now, but in time I am sure it will change

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: but yes i am trying to just stop and leave but sometimes he calls especially when he needs help and I cant not help him, and I get reminded of everything somewhere a long the way

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: okay, in my opinion I believe its best to be strong and try and focus on yourself and live a good life without him for now, until he changes his mind then maybe you re-asses the situation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you will keep trying to do that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: if you can't help him, its best you try and focus on yourself.  no worries, I am here for you.  what are you plans for today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
surprising better when can talk about all this
 and none really just staying indoors

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it is better, yes because then we can we see a better future :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: well, any plans yourself

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: okay, it is important to remember to look out for yourself and do what is good for you, so if he calls and wants help when he doesn't deserve it, sometimes its best to say no.  I have to finish my book today for my book club :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yea, I get that. I just fell guilty not helping him sometimes alright, let me not keep you, Thanks for the help.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: how do you think it will go from here?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Probably block him everywhere and try to occupy myself.

situation: One of my oldest friends is extremely insecure. She's been this way for a long time, but fear and immaturity have made it worse recently. It's become super frustrating to have to constantly reassure her that I love her, and she's really my friend, cause her insecurity makes her doubt anyone could love her. I'm anxious about it.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hi

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm dealing with a lot of frustration and anxiety.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Frustrated and anxious

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh I understand how that can be. Would you like to tell me about it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Ok. I have a friend who I've known a long time, who is one of my closest friends, but who is extremely insecure. She constantly seeks affirmation that I love and care for her, even though she should KNOW I do, since we have been friends for so long.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand how that can become a source of frustration after a while. Would you like to share what pushed it beyond the normal point today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's just been an ongoing thing that has escalated recently because she's so afraid and timid and doesn't believe anyone could really love or care for her. I get sick of having to say that of course I love her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can relate because I also have a close friend that seeks constant reaffirmations.  Have you spoken with her about this?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have. I address it every time it comes up. She always sells herself short. Recently I suggested she could babysit my kids, she agreed, then almost immediately said it was ok if I had someone better in mind, and didn't want her, even though I had asked her! do you talk to your friend about it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sometimes it is very difficult to see yourself through someone elses eyes when you undervalue yourself. I know it may be difficult to remain patient when your friend repeatedly does this, but maybe you could explain to her that it makes you feel like she is mistrustful of your opinion when she second guesses your taste in friends.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I know. Until she finds her self worth, it doesn't matter what any of her friends say to her. I have actually told her that. That it seems like she doesn't trust me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Possibly explaining it in a way she can relate to would be helpful. I did in fact speak with my friend and it helped our relationship quite a bit. We continue to be very close 20 years later.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's frustrating to me and gives me anxiety because I wish I could change her to see herself the way I, and others, do. But I know I can't make her see her self worth. That's something she has to find for herself.  That's good to hear about you and your friend.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can feel your frustration just by our conversation, and I too wish that she could see that you value both her and your friendship enough to reach out to someone that can just listen. Don't forget that your feelings are valid as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know they are. And yes, she's dealing with a lot, but we all are, and it would be so much better for her if she could trust the people who love her to actually love her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sometimes you have to take a step back and fill your own cup.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Indeed. Thank you for your input.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Do you think a break would help you feel better able to deal with the demands of her friendship?
 Do you think a break would help you feel better able to deal with the demands of her friendship?
 It has been my pleasure! I trust you will make the right choice! Best of wishes!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not really, because I'd just worry about her. She needs someone like me to look after her. I'm kind of the Yin to her Yang. Thank you for your time and conversation!

dialog.content:
Hello

I just feel so terribly stuck

I just want my life back. I felt like I was on a really good career path at the first of the year, and now I just don't even know what o do

dialog.content:
I don't want to. If there is a vaccine in the first quarter of 2021 then the company I worked for before this will be able to go back to business, and I've been assured my old job back. 
 I'm just in limbo right now, figuring out how to survive until then and keep from going crazy

Thank you for chatting with me, and helping me earn the maximum bonus for this task! I hope you have a wonderful rest of your day! Good luck in your own endeavors.

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: oh good, well i got inputs to put!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Perhaps you could tell me what brings you joy?

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
between that and creation, i love to express myself. art, mov
ement, building something.....its all just me expressing myself, getting my head into the world

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is beautiful! Keep it up!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: whenever i can :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Right. It's tough because we are all friends, she and I, our husbands and our children.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: oh goodness, that sounds like a difficult situation but at least you have your friends to help you through it

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, they are all very upset with her. I am being the most patient but I don't like it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: As the saying goes, good things come to those who are patient! Time heals almost everything

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I agree. I just feel like the behavior is so destructive and erratic.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe your friend doesn't realized the affect her actions has had on the people around her. Have you tried talking about your feelings with her personally?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have. She's very dismissive.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If she doesn't care about how you feel maybe it would be best if you ended your friendship with her

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have considered that. My husband said the same. It's tough.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes I understand. I believe in you and your strength to overcome this!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for the encouragement! I know my family is what is important but this all just threw me for a loop.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Try focusing on your family then, rather than other people. Maybe that will help you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I think that's what I have to do.
 Thank you for the advice.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Other than that, are you okay?

situation: I have been put into sadness due to pressure from my employer who is threatening to down size the manpower at work. I am really sad because my supervisor has mentioned to me that I am going to be one of those who are going to lose their jobs.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hai

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hai. My name is Tom and i really need your help.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: ok tell me

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am really sad and stressed up knowing that I am soon losing my job due to the effects of corona virus. For sure if I lose my job now I might go to depression because I have a family to take care of.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: ok, don't worry friend,

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Are there any ways you know that could help me convince my boss that that it is not the right time to cut down on manpower?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: lot of people lose the job in this corona time just explain you situation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Incase i lose the job, are there ways that can help me stay out of stress of losing the job? I am worried that losing a job may cause me a lot of harm since my bills depend on my current job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: definitely, you have to try to get the other work don't give up your confident,  belive your self

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am really worried that getting another job during this season of corona is not easy, that is the stress that is almost killing me. Sure I do believe myself.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you know any other self work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not really. Do you happen to know any that can sustain me?

dialog.speaker: supporter

situation: College finals are next week and I have been sic so I gave them been able to study like I wanted to.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hi! Hope you are doing well today How may I assist  you ?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hey! I’ve been better. Just so stressed out! I don’t handle pressure well.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Something is pressuring you and you are stressed due to that. Correct me if I am wrong

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That’s right. I have three final exams next week and I’ve had the flu so I haven’t been able to study at all.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It must be hard for you to prepare for it. I can understand. But there must be a way

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I’m sure I can figure something out. I’ll definitely have to study all weekend. I just really need to pass. Do you know any good tricks to help study?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can easily see your seriousness and dedication for  your studies. That is awesome thing

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks! Education is very important in my family. My dad was a teacher so I’ve always had to live up to high expectations.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you should take a complete rest for 2-3 hours without worrying and then get up and eat something tasty and healthy. Afterwards sit for preparing for your exam. That's what I do and it helps me a lot.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I love that idea! I think I’ll feel much better if I take that time to just relax before I really start working.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There is a famous quote " Worry is a total waste of time. It doesn't change anything. All it does is steal your joy and keep you busy doing nothing."

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Whoever said that sure knew what they were talking about. It’s so true! Any snack recommendations? I’ve got some fruit on hand, but it’s a little boring by itself lol

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I definitely recommend trail mix or chex mix and one of my favorites is banana chips!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don’t think I’ve ever tried banana chips! I’ll have to see if my grocery store has got them. Maybe I can get some delivered by the time I’m ready to study

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
you can find it in any grocery store easily known as  Plantain chips
 hope you are feeling much better now

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks so much! I’ve added them and some of my favorite trail mix to my cart. Should be delivered in 1 hour! I am feeling better. Thanks so much for the advice :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great to know and best wishes for your exams.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you. Enjoy the rest of your day!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you too!

situation: my friend stud me up for a weekend trip cost was then all on me and she has been badmouthing me ever since. Saying I am the bad person because of a voicemail left her
