dialog.content:
my friend stood me up and i went alone on a trip she was to go with me. I eneded up flipping all the cost and she is bad mouthing me she stood me up because she was drunk she has a drinking problem
 not good
 since i told her that drinking is more important to her now she is talking bad about me but she is the one with the problem.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
It sounds like you are very stressed out by the situation. 
 Have you explained to her that her excessive drinking is causing problems?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes but i am the bad one she turns everything around

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Why is she turning everything around on you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
she is now also spreading mean rumors about me and dragging my daughter into this
 I do not know why, maybe so she does not have to admit that she is wrong
 she is like a narzisist

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds horrible especially that she is dragging your daughter into it.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes it is

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Could you suggest she talks about her issues with a specialist?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have done that but she will not admit that she has issues, I hear that she falls of chairs in bars on a regular base. so maybe it is good to not have contact with such an individual

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is good that you have suggested it to her already. Hopefully she will eventually realise.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes thanks for listening

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Correct me if I am wrong, but were
 you meant to go on a trip somewhere with her?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes i was I ended up going alone

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How did you feel about going on the trip on your own? Thank you for sharing your feelings with me today.

situation: I've been experiencing repeated romantic rejections from potential mates.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
hello
how are you today

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not great. I'm feeling pretty down in the dumps.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Im sorry to hear that. how can I help

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm not sure how, exactly. I'm dealing with repeated rejections.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is always tough especially during this time of year and been there myself. What type of rejection  are you experiencing

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Romantic rejection. It seems like every time I make an effort, it's immediately shot down.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hate to ask you this but are you male or female?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Male. Why do you ask?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I ask it changes the perception on how I may help you because male and females deal with things differently.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Fair enough. What do you suggest I try?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am going to ask you a few questions if OK i don't want to sound like i am prying.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's fine. I'm open to discussion.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: OK do yo live in a rural or city, are you into male females or the like?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm in a rural area, and I'm interested in women.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I too live in a rural area understand the complexities of tying to find someone. So my next question where looking to find that special connection.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've had to resort to online dating and such as there are few opportunities to meet people in person.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand doing that I have tried it myself and trying to find that connection is few and far between. What sites have you tried using?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Basically all of the ones you would expect. How would you suggest meeting people in person?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well It would depend on if you were religious I  would suggest church functions for example. I use my hobbies like pool and darts and cards and join groups that creates some common ground.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That sounds like a good idea. Do you attend meetups?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have and i usually take someone i  trust with me and if it doesnt feel right I leave.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's good advice. Thanks for your help.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No problem glad I could help. Is there anything else I can help with?

situation: Not being able to do my daily routine is really getting to me. It's hard to get up every morning.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you doing?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm doing okay.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything you want to talk about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm having problems with getting up everyday and doing my daily routine. I feel bad because these are things that other people do normally

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah, I've definitely felt that way...especially during COVID and having to adjust to working from home. What are some of the things in your routine during the day?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Exactly. Jogging, eating breakfast, getting dressed

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yep, for me it's kind of felt like working remotely and all the crazy 2020 stuff was kind of an excuse to just "take a break" from grinding. MTurk has kind of helped me get back in a working mindset and given me more energy...have you been doing a lot of MTurk lately?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah, 2020 has been a crazy year. I've been doing Mturk
 here and there. I plan on doing it more. It helps me stay productive.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah it helps, just forcing yourself to have goals, for sure. I've been determined to hit a number on MTurk and exercise every time I'm feeling tired, instead of taking a nap because my boss can't see me, lol. I would definitely say just start setting goals and try to look forward to 2021 being better.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay, I hear you. Thanks. Yeah, I guess I have to really force myself.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Force yourself at first, then the "body in motion, stays in motion" law will take over. :) All I know, is that nothing is worse than ending a day without a sense of accomplishment and then stringing those kinds of days together.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay okay. cool. That's a helpful concept. I'm going to force myself to get up and then hopefully my body will get used to it. Yes, that's true. I hate ending the day knowing I've bummed it out

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: One of the things I've noticed about 2020 and I think this contributes is that this year has taken away almost all incentives...you know before it would be like, "I'm looking forward to this concert, or this movie coming out next weekend." So, when you aren't planning things and don't have these milestones throughout the year of events breaking up the monotony, it stinks.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I agree. I used to go to at least one concert every year. Now that's out of the question. It gets boring and a little depressing. Hopefully 2021 will be better.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sure it will be. Definitely try tomorrow morning to get up and exercise and maybe hit the MTurk hard, also hobbies definitely help and being at home is a great time to learn new skills.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will definitely. I just got to get up and get with it. I'm really in need of some exercise so I'll do that too. I used to paint and draw but I kind of fell off and stopped doing it. I'll get back into that too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh yeah, nothing is worse than losing joy in something you love doing. But, getting back in an active mindset helps. I used to love to have bonfires, it really was therapeutic for me but then I just lost the will to have them. Since I've been back on MTurk and trying to be more active these last couple weeks, I had one on Saturday and really enjoyed it. So much of the passion lost for hobbies isn't because we grow out of them, it's quite frankly because we are probably just depressed.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I agree fully and I'm happy for you. I think I've been in some sort of depression for some time now. I stopped doing everything I loved. I used to love jogging but I stopped doing that too. I ended up gaining a lot of weight and now I don't like going anywhere or being seen at all. I'm usually 130 and I've shot up to 200. So I think if I can just get my routine down and exercise, I'll be alright.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yep for sure, just get back at it and learn to love it again. Anyways, good talking to you!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too and thanks for the support!

situation: 7 weeks ago, my lover and I decided to stop having an affair.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi, I am having a tough time processing a correct decision today

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good morning. What type of decision is troubling you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: A few weeks ago, my lover and I decided to stop our affair. It is the right choice for him because he feels he should work on his marriage. I admire that, yet It is very lonely for me right now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand the feeling lonely would make you feel your decision was wrong but from an outsiders view, it was not a healthy relationship if he was already committed to someone.  You deserve something better. Someone dedicated to you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It was a fantastic experience. we were together for 3 years. I am not mad at him, he's a good guy. He was separated when we met. But as all life, it's complicated

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I completely understand. I have been in your shoes before. I learned a lot and found out more about myself in the growing experience after the relationship ended. I'm sure there was some positives even with you at the end of the relationship now.  Each person is put in our life for a reason or season. This may just be the stepping stone to finding your soul mate. The perfect relationship won't feel so tricky in the long run and will make you feel on top of the world!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I had my eyes wide open during the process and he and I were very open with our communication. I can see lots of blessings but I am just so sad. Each day is a challenge to focus  I can't imagine a relationship right now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sounds like you really but your heart in to this and I'm sure it hurts. No need to rush relationship. Falling back in love with yourself is the key first. Finding your inner happiness will make the next relationship that much better and stronger!  Treat yourself to a few solo dates to find joy in the day to day life you are now building without him.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have heard the idea of dating ones self  I haven't been out to eat for over a year due to the pandemic situation

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It can be fun! No decisons on who wants to go where. It is all about you enjoying the moment and your own company!  No need to go out if you feel uncomfortable. Find a movie you have been wanting to watch and order some take out! Or do some online shopping while enjoying your favorite dessert or adult beverage!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: During this time with the affair, I had the strength to leave a narcissistic spouse that abused me emotionally. I am proud of myself for that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a lot to handle! I am proud of you for that as well! No one ever deserves to be abused. Some solo time to rebuild your independance may do you wonders and give you back your strength and self confidence! Seems like the past year has been emotionally draining.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It has been. You are right about me focusing on solo time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think you know yourself better than anyone else and are about to leap in to a new chapter of your life. You received lessons from both the past relationships and can take that forward knowing what you wish for and deserve.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for letting me talk about this. It is a touchy topic, affairs. My heart was so loved during that time but I always told him that if he needed me to step back, I would. It has been difficult on both of us. Being a parent, having the families meshed together, homes, work, etc.... I can understand why he feels he should try again.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sometimes talking about why you are feeling is the best way to find closure. Keep the memories of the love and good times but know that in the end you were destined for something so much more. As adults we try so often to find happiness in others. He is a good person for wanting to try to resolve the situation for his family but that does not mean he did not love you or that your relationship was not a bright spot in his life as it was yours. I'm sure he is hurting too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
thank you so much for saying that. It really means a lot to me and grounds me
 you are correct, that I need to move forward for me. I deserve it. That's a concept I have been learning.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You deserve peace through this transition. It will take some time but I think you will come out even stronger and happier on the long run. Journaling may be a great outlet for your emotions as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you for that idea. He was my muse in that relationship and I would write beautiful stories and poetry for him. Writing out my journey to the next stage of my life would be beneficail

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think so! It may also let you heal while building your creativity even more! You seem like a very passionate and loving person.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You made me heart smile... thank you.. !! I am glad that I got to speak with you today!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are so very welcome! You were a pleasure to talk to and I wish you all the best! Let this year be YOUR year!

situation: I am depressed because I broke up with my girlfriend

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Hello I am very sad 
 I am more depressed than ever

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Would you care to elaborate about what you're feeling sad about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I recently broke up with my girlfriend

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that, that sounds difficult.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
its been very hard on me

 I've spent the last few years wit her

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How have you been coping? That can be really hard, have you been able to talk to anyone about it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
i've been trying to talk to family
 mainly my mom 
 and brother

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you find that to be helpful?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yes
 family is always there for me
 its only hard because I've been with my gf for so long
 and we've spent so much time during quarantine together

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's good, it's great to have a good support system.  I can imagine that's difficult, that would be a lot of close time together.  Have you been able to socialize with friends or colleagues?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
i try to keep my personal life away from my coworkers
 i've talked to a few friends
 i'm actually going out tonight to meet some up for happy hour

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's understandable about the coworkers. Going out sounds like would be beneficial, just to at least spend some time with people who care about you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yea its going to take my mind off of things

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you been able to engage in any other activities that help you cope/take your mid=nd off things?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
i've gone for long runs and swims
 well i'm going to go get ready
 thank you for all your time and advice

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything else I can help you with?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: if you were in my shoes, what would you do if you broke up with a serious gf/bf

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would lean on my friends, family, and others for support such as a therapist (they're good for unbiased talking), I would also focus on hobbies and things that make me feel better. Most importantly be kind to yourself and allow your self to grieve.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
thats great advice
 how long should i wait before i move onto the next potential girlfriend?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That would be up to you, there's no timeline it's when you feel most comforable

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
thats true
 i am getting old

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There's no timeline on being able to date

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yea, my brother and sister whom are both younger than me , are already married with kids
 i feel bad because I'm the oldest one and without a family

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Everyone has their own timeline, some of my friends have married in their late 40s and older

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yea, thats what my family is telling me
 to not rush into thigns

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You have to do what's right for you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
well tahnk you for your time.
 take care.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have a good time with your friends.

situation: Since getting a new job I feel like I'm constantly trying to prove myself. I measure myself against other people and just don't feel I'm good enough I'm having a real issue with my confidence at the moment

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi there

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
This is a tough one. since starting a new job and being around really smart people and feeling that I can't really measure up anymore. I'm finding this really tough, I'm good at my job I'm very committed but I just don't seem to get the same results as
 others?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So it sounds like you feel like you don't measure up to your co-works because they are getting different results than you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that is certainly part of it however I also feel like they have more experience than me so maybe I'm just putting too much pressure on myself?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would completely agree with that statement you are much newer than your co-workers. I believe you that you are very committed to your job. I am sure that with time you will get the same results as them if not better! Are there any co-workers you feel close to or see a friendship developing with?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that's nice of you to say but I also don't feel as organised. I suppose that's to be understood in a new job scenario.  Yes there are a few really nice co-workers

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Of course! With time you will get the hang of it. Well thats good to hear! Maybe you could ask one of them to coffee and express how you are feeling. I would bet that they felt the same when they started.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think what I do is so complicated I sometimes wonder if I've been off more than I can chew. is this something you can relate to?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That certainly is something I can relate. There was a period of time in school where i signed up for way too many classes and had to drop out of a few. If you feel like you are in over your head it is ok to take a step back. It is important that you take care of your self and know your limits. :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I suppose it's difficult to know when something is too much for you or if you need to give it more time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would say if you are showing signs for several weeks of being overwhelmed (tired, stressed, snappy etc.) That is probably is too much

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thanks
he problem is I've worked really hard to get a better position and if I wasn't successful and feel like a failure. maybe I should look at it like this job just isn't for me if, like you say, I can't seem to get on after several weeks.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would say thats the right option if this persists. I am sure there are other jobs that would fit you well!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I suppose that is true actually. Just because I'm not good at this job, doesn't mean I wouldn't be great others. I suppose it's best to give it a go and see at least then you can say you tried. have you ever put too much pressure on yourself?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes i have and it did not end well I burned out and couldn't do my job at all.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Ah, so you have suffered with a similar situation. it is comforting to know the others face similar challenges and it's not just me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Of course you are not alone!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I think I'll give this a good few weeks and ask myself how I think I'm doing then go from there.
 Thanks for the chat and your suggestions.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that is a great idea I wish you luck! Remember no matter what you will find somewhere you belong!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you!

situation: I have been dismissed from medical school. I am currently awaiting the appeals process to begin but this is a life long dream and I failed not only myself but my family.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there. How are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Im feeling a little down

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am really sorry to hear that. Is it something specific that you'd like to talk about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I am being dismissed from medical school

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am really sorry to hear that! Is it related to the pandemic or have you doing badly in your exams?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
i am currently in the appeals process but i havent left the house in a weeks
 yes my child was having suicidal thoughts so my attention went there

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Please try not to worry. Do you have someone to advocate for you in the appeals process?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: no its all on me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think you should look into getting an advocate. You have a legal entitlement to have representation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
because of the holidays it will be kind of pointless 
 they will probably see my cause a few days before the scheduled meeting

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah I do understand. I have been frustrated when things grind to a halt at this time of year too. Can you ask for an extension on your appeal time?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No extensions allowed  but thank you for your suggestions

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds very harsh, especially given the current situation. I suggest that you find out where you stand legally because I am sure that this can't be right

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes i believe so, i am currently looking into alternatives as far as future career goals

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can tell you are feeling really hopeless and anxious right now. Do you have any fun plans for the season?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i am feeling both honestly i do have plans but do not want to go

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it would be a good idea to try to coax yourself into going. You will probably enjoy it when you get there :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
i have been to a few trips visiting family i did not really enjoy myself i want to be alone 
 that is my current problem 
 i think i am depressed

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you considered speaking to your doctor? You might benefit from some medication or counselling.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i am currently on medication i suffer from severe depression

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry. I suffer from depression also and am on medication so I can understand your pain.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thank you 
 its very hard

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Unfortunately the current global situation is not helping any of us. It is terrible to feel scared all the  time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
This is true 
 thank you I feel a little better knowing im not the only one going through this

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Perhaps you could make a list of things that you mean to do in the New Year to give you something to focus on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
thats a good idea actually 
 I use to have a journal but havent wrote in it for years 
 it helped a lot

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Make sure to only choose realistic goals so's not to make yourself feel despondent and do not be too hard on yourself if something does not work out!  A journal is a fantastic idea. You can track your personal progress over a period of time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you youre right i put too much on myself

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Unfortunately, depression does mean that we can judge ourselves far too harshly. I have been guilty of that myself in the past.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes it has bothered me for years and its like a weight on my shoulders

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hope I have been able to be of some comfort to you and given you something to think about. Have a lovely Christmas!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: you too and thank you again

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Take care!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too Merry Christmas

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: (You need to finish from your end .. I can't :))

situation: I recently broke up with my boyfriend and I'm having a hard time because it's almost the holidays.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how can i help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello there, i am having a hard time because my boyfriend and I just broke up

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm so sorry to hear that. Was there any reasons that lead to the decision?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I was feeling overwhelmed by his political views and with the election and everything it became much more obvious that we weren't right for each other. But Im nervous with the holidays coming up that I will be sad to be alone

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I feel you, it's scary to not be with the person you love so suddenly, especially during holiday

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is!! we were together for 5 years, so this is uncharted territory for me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: However, from what you told me, it's no longer fit for you two to be together as your views are opposite. Maybe this is for the better You can plan to spend the holiday with your friends or family instead. We are usually busy and neglect the most important people. This is the time to take care of that

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
That is true, and that was why I broke it off. He supported Trump, which I knew 4 years ago during the election, but this year it was overwhelming. 
 I will be with family thankfully, we have a small family so COVID isn't something I have to worry about.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. You made the right decision, if not now you will get in even more arguments in the future. He is just not the right person

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Overall, I know im going to be ok, but it feels so fresh and like it hurts still.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Spend the time now that you have on yourself too. Be an even better version of you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thank you for vaildating my decision.  Yes, that is  my plan. Focus on me and finding out what it is that I truly want in life. 
 He's helped me understand what I don't want.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's the right attitude. With the right person, you learn what you like. With the wrong person, you learn what to avoid

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Have you ever gone through something like this?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No I have not, but I have someone ghosted on my before. It hurt and actually helps me realize who cares and serious about me

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That can be just as hurtful! I heard something helpful once that if someone is into you youll know it and if you dont know it, then you dont want to be with them anyways.  Or that you shouldn't be with them

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah, it took me quite a long time to break it off, and I'm glad I did. I'm glad you decided to call it off with your ex too, it's not an easy decision

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for all your help!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you too, it's very nice talking to you

situation: I can't cope with Martins any more, he is a big liar

Hello.

he will never tell you, truth, last week we fix an appointment at the park by 4pm, you will not believe it that I waited from 4pm till 6.30pm... he will tell you I will so
on be there  all kinds of lying

dialog.content: he will never tell you truth. If Martins is seeing red like this in your front , he will tell you is white  mmmmh

go there often that is only major thing that still bind us
 I should have let 
he go off my life

I hate lies, it really pain me if a you lie to me 
 yes of cause 
 thanks , for you time

situation: Conflict with parents

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm having issues with my parents. You can say we are having a conflict

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What kind of issues do you think you are having?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
well they won't allow me to date this girl i met online. 
 They told she's bad news

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand it can be very difficult at times with parents. Especially since they lived in a life that was very different from today.  Have you tried talking to you parents about why she is "bad news"?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, i tried but it won't get through their head. There are so old school. They want me to date someone close to my age.  is 15 year difference too big of a gap?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: The basic problem is that your parents believe that 15 years is too large of an age gap between you guys.  Is there any other reasons your parents dissaporve of her?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They told me she's  gold digger.  Yes i come from money but hey this girl is the one.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You believe that the girl is not a gold digger, but instead you guys like each other for who you are. Money is not an issue for you guys?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: nope not at all. hmm well she does ask to use my credit card alot. my parents foot the bill.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Perhaps you should try to talk to your parents around this fact. For example, if money is the problem for your parents, you can set some rules. LIke you can set an allowance for how much money you can use each week.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Ok, i will talk to them this weekend. what's better on the phone or in person?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If you want a better communication, I believe in person will be best.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hate talking to my parents face to face, all they do is yell and i get frustrated

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I apologize and I do agree it is sometimes difficult. Perhaps it will be better to talk to them with another  person present.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's a great idiea. i will bring my g.f or is that a bad idea?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If another person is present, it may make it easier. However, I believe it should be someone that is not invovled in this issue. Perhaps another family member or a close family friend.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: gotcha. i will bring my good friend. i'm sure he can help me out. great advice by the way. i'm so mad though why my parents care who i date. man i'm old enough. let me make my choices

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Parents have a difficult time understanding their child at times. Hopefully, through this you all can become closer.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you it will. will talk to them this weeked with a friend present. wish me luck :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I personally understand how difficult this can be. But if this works out. You can have a good family relationship and maintain a good relationship with your girlfriend. Best of luck!

situation: I think I need to break up with my girlfriend because my feelings have changed

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there how can I help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have been in a relationship for just over two years. At the start it was fun and we got along great. I think we have little in common these days and I don't feel the same way I used to. I don;t want to hurt her feelings but I'm thinking about ending the relationship.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you been feeling this way for a while or is it a surprise change?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have felt like this for a while now but I'm thinking about if more nowadays. It doesn't feel right and I don't know who to handle it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you have felt for a while that things aren't right in your relationship and you don't know how to end the relationship without hurting your girlfriend's feelings?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's correct yes. I worry that she will be very upset and the thought of her in distress does concern me. It might be easier to stay together but I don't think that's the right thing to do.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can tell that you are a nice person that cares for your girlfriend and you want the best for her. I think you want to be honest with her which is a good sign of maturity and integrity.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for that. I do care for her which is why I don't want to upset her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Unfortunately this may be upsetting in the short term. I had a friend who had a similar issue. He wanted to end things with his girlfriend and they ended up having an honest conversation, which was upsetting at the time but they are still friends to this day and parted on good terms.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is good that it is possible to do this and still become friends afterwards. I do worry about how this would go and am fearful that she will react badly. How would you go about it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think the best thing to do would just be honest about your feelings and start the conversation with her. I think you could also consider when is the right time to do this so that you minimise the affect that this may have on her.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm not sure what you mean by the right time. Is there ever a right time to do this?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: People who have a negative experience at a key point in their lives, such as a birthday of Christmas, tend to remember that bad experience the following year. I mean choose an appropriate time to have the discussion.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Ok I get it. Don't dump her on her birthday LOL. So I should just start the talk then, may be one evening?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes when she is available to have a good talk about this. Do you have my friends that you can talk to about this?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do have some friends that know how I am feeling yes. Maybe they can help me come up with a plan off what to say.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it's best to be honest and if it comes from the heart then you can at least say you have been honest with yourself and your girlfriend.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think this is going to be hard but thanks for the suggestions

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think you can do this and have confidence that you will do it in the right way. These things are not easy but I know you can ride to the challenge and do this tactfully and with care.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for the kind words. I'll give this a try.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are very welcome.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Ok bye

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Take care

situation: I have dealt with depression around this time of the year since i was 17.  I am now 37.  My cousin got killed in a car accident on Dec 29th  at the age of 14.  Then 18 years later on Jan 3rd I lost my boyfriend in a car accident.  I struggle around the holidays even worse now than I  did before.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am doing ok.  How are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm doing well, thanks for asking. What are you seeking support for today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have been struggling for motivation to do anything the past couple weeks.  This is a really hard time of year for me.  I lost my cousin in a car accident at the age of 14 in a car accident coming up on 20 years this year.  We were very close.  I was 17 at the time.  Her was an alcoholic and my parents raised her a majority of her life.  Then 3 years ago 5 days after her death anniversary I lost my boyfriend at the age of 32 in a car accident.  I dread the holidays.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you for sharing that. It sounds like you are describing a strong association between the holiday season and loss of loved ones. This leads to less of a desire to engage in typical holiday events. Does that sound right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You are exactly right.  The year my cousin got killed her mom was hungover so I went and picked up my cousin and her little brother to take them to our grandparents house for Christmas dinner.  She died 4 days later on Dec 29th.  And my boyfriend it was his first day back to work after the New Year.  He died on Jan 3rd.  I usually go all out for the holidays to try to hide my depression every year.  This year I just can't bring myself to enjoy them.  I have even bought one christmas present.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can see why this time of year is difficult for you. Trying to hide depression can be a challenge. Rather than hiding the sadness or grieving feelings, some people find relief in acknowledging and sharing these feelings with family members who may relate. Do you ever share these feelings with your cousin or grandparents that you mentioned?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My cousin doesn't live around here and we have never been real close.  He is alot younger than me.  We just never had the connection like his sister and I did.  And both my grandparents have passed away.  Grandpa has been gone for 12 years and Grandma has been gone for 4 now. My family has always been really good about talking about our loses. Things just will never be the same.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. When losses are experienced it is a great change in many aspects of our lives.  Are there any aspects of the holidays that bring you joy? Maybe holiday music, movies, bright snow (if there is snow where you live), or seeing joy on someone's face when you give them a great gift?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They say it gets easier. And I agree it does.  Things just have changed so much.  I miss how life was before I lost them.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand - and it is normal to feel that way. When we lose someone who brought us happiness, it is only natural to miss them and their company.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I love giving gifts.  And I always put lots of thought into the gifts I give.  My nieces and nephews can't wait every year to see what I got them.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great! You must have a special talent for personalizing your gift selections. Perhaps focus on that reaction from you nieces and nephews to support your motivation for giving gifts this year.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: This year I am tie dying hooded sweatshirts.  I make tshirts for a hobby.  And to tell you the truth thats the only gifts I have gotten so far.  My oldest daughter turned 18 this year and lives with her boyfriend so its just me and my 15 year old daughter.  She struggles with depression/anxiety as well.  Her dad has been in jail for some stupid actions since August.  She has had to deal with him being an addict pretty much her whole life.  I think we are both missing how the holidays were with her sister still living at home.  Too much change in our lives in such a little time frame.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow, what a great gift idea! That is a solid start on your gifts for the season, and something to be proud of.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They will love them.  A couple years ago I put their names on fleece blankets when I worked at an embroidery shop.  They absolutly loved them!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And I see, it sounds like the changes in your life are potentially impacting your daughter as well. However, I'm glad you have each other for support this season. This year has been crazy for many reasons, and many people tend to find comfort in routines or traditions. Do you and your daughters have any holiday traditions you typically complete? Awesome gift idea! Personalized gifts are always so heartfelt and appreciated. Good thinking!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Just the normal traditions.  My oldest daughter will be here for Christmas morming.  Then we go to my parents.   Thank you for chatting with me tonight!  I hope you have a good rest of your night.

situation: My new supervisor hates me.  She already hated me before she was my boss. In addition to being a terrible manager, she has made a personal goal to inhibit my ability to do my job.  She has taken from me all parts of my job that I value.  This upsets me personally and I am sad & angry that the rest of my team, who I care about, is no longer supported.  We have no recourse to solve this problem.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there, how is life treating you at the moment?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not too bad outside of work.  I'm miserable there.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you are experiencing problems at work? Please tell me about it, I am happy to listen.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Things have changed and my new supervisor has crushed my spirit by taking all the parts I love away.  She never liked me and now she gets to make me suffer.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am very sorry to hear that - trouble with workmates, especially those who are in a higher position, can make things very miserable can't they?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They really can.  I used to love my job and my team.  Now she has made everyone miserable because she just doesn't care about learning the job or the job at all.  My morale is low and worse because I can't help my peers who are struggling as well.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you considered going over her head and putting in a complaint? Especially if she is making everyone else miserable as well. Someone more senior might be able to do something.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have considered this many times.  Unfortunately, the next level manager doesn't respect or value my team in general.  She has made that clear.  She's also just awful and uncomfortable to talk to.  I've found her to just be mean sometimes.  It just feels so hopeless.  Just waiting for someone to notice.  Just hoping she fails.  It's just really hard to show up everyday.  But I do.  Although the days are long and exhausting with no motivation to perform.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Please try to stay positive - bullies never prosper in the long term. I think that you need to talk to your co-workers about this and see if you can come to some sort of arrangement between you  Alternatively, have you ever considered seeking another job? I know that that might feel like selling out but you might be happier somewhere else

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You are right.  She is a bully.  I have called her several names to myself, but not a bully yet.  My coworkers and I have discussed what options we may have.  My suggestion was that we work at our normal pace rather than scrambling to make up for her failures.  I wouldn't want to set her up, but I think her failing is the only way we will ever see a solution.  I have applied for another job within my company.  There seems to be interest in me, so that's exciting.  It is a job I really want.  But then, of course, I feel bad about leaving my peers behind.  I really care about them.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I feel that that is fair enough. You should not be working to make up for her failures and I hear you saying that you feel that this is w hat is happening?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Definitely. Everyone is struggling.  I am the one my team expects to help with some of the tasks she is not performing.  I'm happy to do it for my team, but there is no recognition.  But I think I have been giving them good advice about hanging in there, staying positive, etc...  I should probably listen to myself.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: (sorry was afk give me a second to catch up :))  I really feel that you have grounds for a formal complaint. Just her being incapable is bad enough but bullying too is unacceptable

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You may be right.  My company has an anonymous reporting system.  I should learn a little more about how that works and see if that's an option.  I'm not sure things could get much worse at this point.  This could be my last ditch effort.   That's good advice and you saying it directly to me makes me think I should lean into it.  I can't just spin my wheels in misery much longer.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Anonymous reporting sounds like a good thing, as long as it's not being abused. This sounds like a very good time to use it though  I hope I have been able to give you some good advice here!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Most definitely. I feel more confident now about the anonymous reporting.  I'll gather some notes from my peers to ensure I have a viable complaint.  That's so helpful.  Thank you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are most welcome, enjoy your day!

situation: I lost my 9 -5 job last week, and it makes me feel anxious and fearful, because the pandemic is a serious tough time, and losing a ob in this period is scary

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there, is there anything I can help you with?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hello, thanks for reaching out to me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're most welcome! How are things in your life right now?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: not so good

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything specific that is making you feel bad?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, i lost my 9 - 5 job last week, and considering the pandemic, it makes me anxious and scared

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow that is not good at all. Do you have any savings that you can fall back on in the short term?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes i have some savings , but it is not much

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I believe that there are some government assistance programmes for people who have been made unemployed as a result of the pandemic.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: okay great! do you know how i an access it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am not totally sure but you could try asking your local Citizen's Advice Bureau and they should be able to help.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Alright, no problem, also because of the job loss i have been having insomnia and anxiety problems

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hear you, I have been suffering badly from them the last year too. Have you seen anyone about it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Talked to few friends about it, but their advices does not help

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you considered seeing someone professional? You might be able to get that free too given your circumstances

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i have not seen any professional, do you think that will help?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes I think it should. They might be able to give you advice about your finances too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay great. but have you ever lost a job or know a friend that lost one

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have yes but I was fortunate enough that I was able to get a new one very quickly. I know that there is much more competition for jobs during this time though

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: okay that is very good for you, i am happy for you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you started looking for a new job?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes i have, i have submitted my CV to different companies, i am expecting that they call for interview

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well that sounds very promising. I hope that you are successful.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you so much for that assurance, i really needed it It means a lot

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're most welcome and I wish you all the best in the future.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you very much, i wholeheartedly appreciate!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: :) You need to quit from your end and take the survey - I can't do it my end

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay i will do it right away. Have a GREAT Day!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too!  Just press quit and fill out the survey :)

situation: I need to make more money to pay off my credit cards.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there. How are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm  good. Getting cold outside. Temp dropping over 20 degrees. Brr

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I know! It's supposed to snow this week where I am.  So, I understand you are in need of someone to talk to. What's going on.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Oh no, I hate driving in the snow. Spun out one year- rather not drive in snow- at least when it's icy.
 My interest rate hit another credit card. Have to pay $80 more a month now
 Hoping to find more work so I can pay it off faster

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah, so if I understand you correctly, your interest rate has increased, so you need to find another job to pay it off?

dialog.speaker: seeker

Yeah it's easier said than done. Btw I heard Biden might forgive student loans! Yay!
 Yeah cuz it bought a lot of items I needed so I'm happy I got them

Perhaps if you are unable to find another job to reduce the principal, you could try setting aside a certain amount of money each month to put towards the amount owed?

maybe they will reimburse you on what you paid

I get some but I spend the money on myself- like chocolate!

dialog.content: I understand that also. There needs to be some sort of balance. However, it sounds like you are a smart person and will make sure to pay off the amount responsibly. Everything will be okay!

dialog.speaker: supporter

situation: Not having enough friends nearby.  My three nearby friends went away for holidays, so I have no one to spend time with over this weekend.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello how are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm, okay, thanks, and you?  I'm feeling a bit down because I'm alone for the holidays.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well that's no good, does your family live far away?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, my family is scattered all over the country, like Seattle, Georgia, PA.  I'm sure I'm not the only one with no plans this weekend, though.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: True but I can understand that, it is hard being alone at the holidays. Did you have plans this weekend?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No.  My nearby friends all went away for the holidays.  I could probably just occupy my time with things I need or want to do in order to distract myself.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What kind of hobbies do you have?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm teaching myself the piano.  I'm also teaching myself  how to work a stenography machine for possible future employment as a stenographer.  I also like to read novels.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have never understood how those work..I find it amazing someone can type out whole words or sentences with one word

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, it's pretty cool.  For example, if I hit DRAT in one stroke, then "Did there come a time" will appear in English on my machine's  LCD screen.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow that is crazy, I don't understand how that works, is it preprogrammed or does it go by how the word sounds?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Each stenographer has their own "Personal Dictionary" which is a list of words in English along with how you in particular write out those words or phrases in steno.  This dictionary is present within the actual steno machine after you transfer it from your laptop to the machine via cable or wifi.  Thus your machine then knows how you write out words in particular and how to translate those words from steno to English - each stenographer writes a little differently.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: OK that makes sense, so you basically program it as the user. I take it your stenography machine becomes pretty close to you lol Is this your first holiday alone?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, exactly!  And it's very important to back up your personal dictionary to the cloud or a seperate drive so that you don't lose it in a hard-drive crash.  A lot of work goes into creating that dictionary!  No, it's happened before, being along for the holidays; but you've helped to cheer me up, so thanks!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No problem, I hope I have made your day a little better!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You have, thanks!  Did you do anything special?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have a good rest of this year and don't forget to make your New Year's resolution!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will do that - you too.  Thanks, again...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Not really, stayed home lol... Have a good one!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too... bye now...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good day! What else can I help you with today?

situation: I came off of my anti depressants and regret the decision.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello! How are you tonight?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm okay i guess. But I know my night will get worse as it goes on

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You think your night is going to have emotional upset? Do you fear you will not be physically safe?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know i'll be safe.. but I have depression and it affects me the most at night, every night. I feel like i can't escape it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I'm sorry to hear that. I have struggled to deal with depression most of my life, so I know it can be debilitating, but I have found ways to help myself.
 Is there a specific problem making you depressed, or life in general?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'd love to hear them. There is one problem in general i'm having that i seem to have always had because of the Depression. I have an extremely difficult time falling asleep.. and whenever I do manage to fall asleep.. i wake up always feeling like crap. I never feel rested no matter how long i sleep.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I have had trouble sleeping in the past and sometimes I still do, usually if it is when I'm nervous about the future.
 The number one way I have had to help my depression is to pray and read scripture. I don't know if you are a Christian, but that has helped me a lot. The second way is to have a good friend who will listen, not someone who will TELL me what to do or judge me, but someone who can empathize with me. A good listener is worth her weight in gold.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've learned that mines not really an emotional thing. I feel like my sleeping issue causes my depression. Because no matter what- i always feel exhausted. Nothing has helped it besides medication. It just sucks always feeling fatigued and energyless. I actually did take up religion here this year to help with the coping that comes with this. It was very helpful for a while there

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
If I didn't get good rest, then I would definitely feel tired the next day and over time, it would wear me out. I believe medication will help with the symptoms, but it is important to get the root of the problem, why exactly you find it difficult to sleep.
 Sometimes I take a benydryl because they always make me sleepy, but I usually know why I need to relax, I'm nervous about the next day. So I have found it important to know WHY I don't rest well, if that makes sense to you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I plan on seeing a sleep doctor soon! Sometimes I lose sight of fixing the issue and need to be reminded. And yes!! I was treating my sleeping with Benadryl as well but it wasn't healthy to keep doing that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm also thinking there are a few practical ways you can help yourself sleep. I was advised to get soft sheets, make my sleeping area more restful, make sure the time before bed is relaxing and calming.  I also was helped a lot by a Christian Preacher, he's long dead, but he has audio on youtube -- Frank Hammond, Pigs in the Parlor. It's a 5 part series I found helpful, you might want to jot that down.  And I do encourage you to find a counselor, once you rule out the physical (doctor) then you can look at emotional, mental and/or spiritual. I think most of everything is spiritual and emotional to some degree, and our body just responds to that.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I always lose sight of how to make things better. It really helps to talk sometimes so someone can help me put things into perspective. I'll look into Frank Hammond! I am actually religious and would love to look into that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I will definitely pray for you tonight because I know losing sleep is hard to deal with. But please have hope, sometimes we go through these things to possible get to deeper issues that might need to be dealt with. We only have a few minutes here, but I'm pretty old and I have found this to be true so many times.  Get a good support system of good friends. Having people who "get" you is SO vital to our lives. And learning to be grateful helps too. And especially journal, find out what may be bothering you.
 I had some abuse issues and that is why I had a hard time resting. I also would wake up in the middle of the night and obsess over problems with people. I had a lot to work through, but most nights, I sleep restful now. It may take time.
 I have hope for you! :D

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That sounds very similar to my situation! I always had some abuse issues. Thank you so much . Talking to you made me feel a lot better

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh, sometimes I would drink Sleepy Time Tea, be kind to yourself. God bless, I'm glad I could encourage you. Sometimes it is really hard to push through difficult times!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'll look into that tea! Thank you so much. God bless

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thanks you too! We are all blessed when we help each other! Have a great night.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it's really great that you're doing all that for her! It must be extra hard to have to limit public interactions even more than most people. dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you don't think the online interactions are a good substitute for in-person things. Does that sound right?  Ah, that sounds like kind of a cycle of guilt going on. Where she feels bad about you not being able to go out, and you feel bad about her feeling bad.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, it does. I don't need a lot of in-person interaction. I'm an introvert, so this was all just fine when it started. But even introverts need to be around people sometimes.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I really hope things get better for you. Covid should be ending soon hopefully with the vaccine, and things will start to go a bit back to normal

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Yeah! I hope that works out for you. I've been finding getting outside and doing things with others has been helpful both for social and exercise. 
 It's been really nice chatting with you :)

dialog.content: You too!
